column1: [DAILY] Automation Regression Health Check (V2 & V3) – 7:30 AM Triage & Reporting - 04/01-04/14
column34:
Every business day at *7:30 AM* (local to offshore), perform a *daily health check* of the *Prime V2* and *Prime V3* regression jobs that finished overnight.
Tasks include: review *qTest* executions and *TestNG* reports, scan failures *by module*, reproduce locally or run a focused re-execution to confirm legitimacy, and communicate results.

* *If all green:* post a short status update to the team channel.
* *If failures:* email the Project Lead (Swapnil) following the bug-handling guidelines with the required evidence (plan, module, test case(s), exception, logs/screenshots, links). Swapnil will open/route defects.

*Goal:* Provide a reliable, single source of truth each morning on regression health (V2 + V3), with actionable failure details.

*Linked Docs:*

* *Bug Handling Process:* [https://confluence.ascensus.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=326751557|https://confluence.ascensus.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=326751557]
* *TestNG (V2) Report Index:* [http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/?C=M;O=D|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/?C=M;O=D]
* *V3 Pipelines (TestNG inside job artifacts):* [https://gitlab.com/ascensus-gs/products/depot/qa-automation/prime-test-automation/-/pipeline_schedules|https://gitlab.com/ascensus-gs/products/depot/qa-automation/prime-test-automation/-/pipeline_schedules]
* *qTest Portal (Project 90985):* [https://ascensus.qtestnet.com/p/90985/portal/project#tab=testexecution|https://ascensus.qtestnet.com/p/90985/portal/project#tab=testexecution]

column1: MID Stage 1 – legacy member login URL returns Page not found; CSR Update Password link missing; V2 regression / IDP transition alignment (03/30/2026)
column34:
During *V2 Stage 1* web login regression on *03/30/2026*, *MID*-specific failures were observed: the *legacy member login URL* ({{https://mid.stage1.acs529.com/midtpl/auth/ll.cs}}) returns *Page not found*, and in *CSR* for MID the *Update Password* link is *not visible*. *Other plans* in the same run *work as expected*, indicating *MID-specific* behavior or configuration.

*Hypothesis for triage*
Legacy member login may be *deprecated or replaced by IDP* for MID. If so, *defect vs. expected cutover* must be confirmed with *product/platform owners*. *QA Automation* may need to *stop using MID in legacy-login suites* (replace with plans still on legacy member login) and *cover MID under IDP login* suites instead.

*Environment*

* *Environment:* Stage 1
* *Plan:* MID
* *Suite context:* V2 regression – web login (e.g. {{stage1-web-login}})

*Observed behavior*

* Legacy MID login URL: *Page not found*.
* CSR (MID): *Update Password* link *absent*.

*Expected behavior (if legacy still supported)*
Legacy MID login page loads; CSR shows *Update Password* for MID consistent with other legacy-enabled plans.

*Expected behavior (if IDP is intended)*
Document *supported entry points*; *automation and manual TCs* updated to *IDP*; legacy URLs removed from active regression or marked obsolete.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Navigate to {{https://mid.stage1.acs529.com/midtpl/auth/ll.cs}} on Stage 1.
# Observe page not found (or equivalent).
# Access CSR for MID and confirm Update Password link presence/absence.

h3. Priority / Severity (suggested)

* *Priority:* High – Blocks MID login/password-related regression and may block sign-off for MID until scope is clarified.
* *Severity:* Major if legacy is still required; *Process/TC* if behavior is *intentional IDP cutover* pending suite/TC updates.

h3. Attachments / Links

* *TestNG report:* [http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20260330/stage1-web-login/#|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20260330/stage1-web-login/#]

column1: Automatic Dollar Cost Averaging for CSR Direct plan
column573:
h3. Scope (this feature file)

CSR completes *Automatic Dollar Cost Averaging* for a member account (with outbound/inbound fund allocation per test data), then automation *persists and validates* the financial instructions in the same way as the existing annual-exchange validation path.

----

h3. Preconditions

* CSR can open the configured *CSR login* URL and run with *browser + Unite DB* as in the Background.
* *Test cases* load from the associated CSV (e.g. {{AutomaticDollarCost.csv}}) with valid {{USERNAME}}, {{PASSWORD}}, account mapping from *pull account information*, and fields such as {{OUTGOING_FUNDS}}, {{INCOMING_FUNDS}}, {{FORCE_EXISTING}}, {{FUTUREALLOC}}, {{INCOMING_FUND_*}}, {{REASON_CODE}}, {{TRANS_CHANNEL}} as required by the harness.

----

h3. CSR journey (Background + first steps)

# CSR *logs in* using the test case credentials.
# CSR *pulls account information* so the scenario account is available.
# CSR *jumps to the member account* by account number from test data.

----

h3. Automatic Dollar Cost flow (*When* – high level)

# From member servicing, CSR opens *Change investment options* and starts *Automatic Dollar Cost Averaging* (DCA entry point), not the one-time “Exchange now” annual-exchange entry (unless product maps them intentionally).
# When shown, CSR can complete *source portfolio* selection (automation may pick a random option when multiple {{srcPortfolioId}} radios exist).
# After fund allocation steps, when shown, automation may set *run frequency* (e.g. M/Q/H/Y), *run day* (1–31), and *stop date* consistent with product rules (automation: *20 months after* parsed start on {{form_startDate}}, or *today* if start is missing—per your step implementation).
# *Outgoing* fund instructions are created per test data ({{createAllMoneyOutInstructions}}).
# If {{REASON_CODE}} is required (not {{N}}), a *Reason Code* is supplied.
# *Incoming* allocations are applied per test data (including {{FORCE_EXISTING}} and fund-type rules), using *percent*-style allocation consistent with the annual-exchange validation ({{INCOMING_TXN_TYPE}} / {{OUTGOING_TXN_TYPE}} = PERCENT in validation).
# CSR proceeds through *Next*, handles *large-amount* popup when balance > 50k, *skip class* (advisor) and *transaction channel* (ABLE CSR prompt) when applicable, *submits*, confirms *invoice* prompt if shown, and finishes with *Done*.

column1: Fix for Custom exchange for Csr
column573:
# *Locator resolution* — {{clickElement}} for the *first Continue* after outgoing instructions uses a locator that *always resolves* in Prime’s page map (e.g. shared {{Continue Button}} from {{common_element_locators.txt}}), so runs do not fail with *Element locator missing* before interacting with the DOM.
# *CSR locator file* — {{Continue Button Custom Exchange}} in {{csr_element_locators.txt}} uses a *valid, parseable* XPath string (no stray quotes/brackets); optional: aligns with real markup ({{input[@name='buttonClicked']}}, {{@value='Continue']}}, etc.).
# *Common locator backup* — {{Continue Button Custom Exchange}} exists in {{common_element_locators.txt}} with a correct XPath so the name resolves even if CSR-only files are not on the classpath or load order differs.
# *Runtime behavior* — On a representative environment (e.g. Direct/NYD CSR custom exchange), the *Continue* control is *clicked* after outgoing funds (no locator-missing assertion); flow proceeds to large-amount popup (if applicable), incoming funds, second Continue, Submit.
# *Regression* — CSR *Custom Exchange* scenarios that previously failed at the first Continue (locator missing) *pass* with the same test data and tags (e.g. {{@regression}} / {{@dailyrun}} as configured).
# *Build* — If tests run from {{unite-automation.jar}}, the updated locators and step defs are included in the built artifact used for the run.

column1: Fix for Annual Exchange for Direct plan for member Portal
column573:
h2. 1. Annual Exchange — *Member portal*, *Direct plan*

*Scope:* Logged-in member completes an *annual exchange* on a *Direct* plan account from *investment / change investments* (same journey your member automation uses: e.g. {{AnnualExchange.feature}} with member login and Investment Options / MFA where applicable).

*Acceptance criteria*

# *Access* — Eligible member can open *Change investments* (or equivalent) and start *Annual exchange* without error for a Direct-plan test account.
# *Outgoing* — Member can define *money out* per plan rules (e.g. percent/units/dollar per testcase) for the configured number of source funds.
# *Incoming* — Member can allocate *money in* to the target fund(s) per testcase (including multi-fund and age-based/static variants if in scope).
# *Future allocations (if UI presents it)* — Where the flow includes *future contribution* allocation choice, member can select *Yes* or *No* per testcase and proceed.

column1: IDP Login fails on submit for most plans on Stage 1 – NYD works; Forgot Username/Password OK (03/25/2026)
column34:
Stage 1 *IDP login* automation is failing *on submit* for *nearly all plan-specific login tests* with a *consistent failure pattern*. *NYD* IDP login *passes*. *Forgot Username* and *Forgot Password* (and similar ancillary flows) *continue to pass*, indicating the defect is likely scoped to the *primary login submit* path or *plan-specific login configuration*, not the entire IDP stack.

*Environment*
Stage 1 · Prime V3 ({{unite-universal-enrollment}} / IDP Login suite as run in nightly regression).

*Observed behavior*

* Submit on IDP login fails for *most plans* (same issue pattern).
* *NYD* IDP login *works*.
* *Forgot Username / Forgot Password* flows *work*.

*Expected behavior*
Successful authentication after valid credentials and submit for all plans covered by regression, consistent with NYD behavior.

*Actual behavior*
Widespread login submit failures except NYD; ancillary IDP flows still pass.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Execute IDP Login regression (or manually test) for a *non-NYD* plan through credential entry and *Submit*.
# Observe failure (_detail per exception/UI_).
# Execute same for *NYD* — observe *success*.
# Run Forgot Username / Forgot Password — observe *success*.

h3. Priority / Severity (suggested)

* *Priority:* High — Broad regression failure across IDP login; blocks nightly confidence for most plans.
* *Severity:* Major — Core login path broken for multiple plans; workaround limited (NYD only not representative of full suite).

h3. Attachments / Links

* Screenshot: {{10_IMPORTS_RAW/regression_reports/03252026/IDP Login failure on submit.png}}
* TestNG report: _[PASTE GitLab Pages job URL –_ {{.../surefire-reports/index.html}}_]_
* Exception file(s): _[PASTE link(s) to_ {{*_IDPLogin*exception_failedresult.txt}} _for one representative failing plan]_

h3. Labels / Tags (suggested)

{{idp}}, {{login}}, {{regression}}, {{stage1}}, {{prime-v3}}, {{multi-plan}}, {{submit}}

h3. Components (suggested)

* IDP Login / Authentication
* Plan-specific routing or configuration (if known after triage)
column724: Root cause was a caching issue on Stage 1. Rebuild, deploy, and server restart cleared the bad state; IDP login submit is working again across affected plans. No application or automation code change was required.

column1: Future Exchange for Investment Allocation for   Exchange 2 Funds to 4 Funds
column573:
CSR login — User can sign in with testcase credentials (USERNAME / PASSWORD from CSV).
Account resolution — System selects an account with exactly 2 current funds (OUTGOING_FUNDS=2, EXACT_FUNDS=Y), exchange-limit eligible (LIMIT_EXCHANGE=Y), and stores ACCOUNTID (same pattern as other CSR investment flows in CreationStepDefs).
Jump to member — CSR can open that account using account number search and reach account view.
Transaction flow (Future exchange)

Entry — From Change Investments, user starts via Allocations for Future Contributions (not Annual Exchange Now).
Outgoing — Outgoing instructions cover all funds on the account (for a two-fund account, both funds are included; amounts are built as 100% per fund in createAllMoneyOutInstructions).
Incoming — User can direct proceeds into four destination funds shown on the page, with percent allocations that total 100% (INCOMING_FUNDS=4 → createMoneyInInstructions with PERCENT and 100).
Not “existing only” — FORCE_EXISTING is not Y, so incoming funds are chosen from the normal page/available-fund logic, not the “force existing” path.
Reason code — With REASON_CODE=N, no reason code is required in the UI.
CSR channel (if prompted) — If “CSR: You must capture the caller information” appears, user selects Primary Account Owner from testcase TRANS_CHANNEL.
Completion — User can proceed (Next), Submit, and Done without an Equity Wash Sale hard stop.

column1: Future Exchange for Investment Allocation for   Exchange 2 Funds to 3 Funds
column573:
CSR login — A CSR user can log in using the credentials from the testcase (USERNAME / PASSWORD in CSV).

Account selection — The framework resolves an account that matches the testcase rules: OUTGOING_FUNDS=2, EXACT_FUNDS=Y (exactly two funds on the account), and LIMIT_EXCHANGE=Y (exchange-eligible per helper logic), then stores ACCOUNTID for later steps 

Navigate to member — From CSR navigation, the user can jump to the member account by account number using the pulled ACCOUNTID, search by account, and land in the account view 

Future exchange transaction

Correct entry point — On Change Investments, the user enters the flow with Allocations for Future Contributions (not Annual Exchange Now) 

Outgoing instructions — Outgoing instructions are generated for all funds on the selected account (for a two-fund account this is effectively full exchange out of both funds, each at 100% as built by createAllMoneyOutInstructions; .

Incoming instructions — User can allocate proceeds into three incoming funds listed on the page, using percent allocations that sum to 100% (INCOMING_FUNDS=3), without forcing “existing fund only” (FORCE_EXISTING not Y)

No separate annual “future allocations” radio on this path — The Annual Exchange Future Allocations yes/no control is not exercised in this flow (that block is commented out in fillFutureExchangeFlow).

Reason code — With REASON_CODE=N, no reason code is entered.

CSR caller / channel (if shown) — If the “CSR: You must capture the caller information” message appears, the user selects the transaction channel from the testcase — Primary Account Owner for this row 

Submit & complete — User can advance (Next), Submit, and finish with Done without hitting an Equity Wash Sale blocking message.

column1: ABLE Exchange for Exchange 2 Static Funds to 1 Static Funds
column34: ABLE Exchange for Exchange 2 Static Funds to 1 Static Funds
column573:
*Scope:* Member logs into the Member portal and performs an *Annual Exchange* (reallocate existing balances from current funds to other funds). This is the “Change investment options” flow for the member.

----

h3. 1. Account selection / pull

* System can pull account details for the test case (account with funds and balance suitable for exchange).
* No “Failed to find account” (or equivalent) for the scenario.

h3. 2. Login and navigation

* Member logs in with test credentials for the test case.
* Member can open *Change investment options* (e.g. “Change investment options” link).
* Member can start *Annual Exchange Now* from that page.

h3. 3. Outgoing allocation

* Current funds and balances are shown.
* Member can define *100% money-out* from the required outgoing fund(s) (by percent, totalling 100% from those funds).
* Outgoing instructions are created from the account’s current funds.

h3. 4. Incoming allocation

* Available funds for incoming allocation are displayed (e.g. “Annual Exchange Funds”).
* Member can allocate *100%* into one or more incoming funds *by percentage*; total incoming = 100%.
* Behavior respects test data: *INCOMING_FUNDS*, *FORCE_EXISTING* (Y = use only existing funds), *INCOMING_FUND_TYPE* (e.g. Age-Based, Static) when applicable.

h3. 5. Future allocation

* When the plan supports it (and traunch is not RIA), member can set *Future allocation* to Yes or No per test data (*FUTUREALLOC*).
* If *FUTUREALLOC=Y*, future contributions use the new allocation.

h3. 6. Reason code

* When *REASON_CODE* is required (not {{N}}), member can select a reason and continue.

h3. 7. Plan-specific prompts

* *Large amount:* If account balance > 50,000, any “Large Amount” confirmation can be acknowledged and the flow can continue.
* *Advisor:* If “Annual Exchange Skip Class” (or similar) is shown, it can be selected and the exchange can proceed.
* *ABLE:* If “You must capture the caller information” is shown, *Transaction Channel* (e.g. Primary Account Owner) can be set per *TRANS_CHANNEL*.

h3. 8. Submission

* Member can submit the exchange successfully (no blocking validation or system errors).
* If “This transaction will result in an invoice” appears, member can confirm (e.g. Submit again) and complete the flow.
* Member can click *Done* to finish.

h3. 9. Equity wash

* If the *Equity Wash Sale rule* is triggered, the system blocks or warns appropriately and the test can fail with a clear message.

h3. 10. Database and instruction validation (when automated)

* *log and validate annual exchange* (when step is enabled):
** **TU_FIN_TXN

h3. 5. Advisor-specific behavior

h3. 6. Future allocation

* *Future allocation* is set per test data (Yes/No) when the plan supports it.

h3. 7. Submission

* Transaction *submits successfully* (no validation or system errors).
* Any reason code or confirmation/invoice popup is handled as per test data.

h3. 8. Database and instruction validation

* *log and validate annual exchange* passes:
** *TU_FIN_TXN* / *TU_FIN_INSTRUCTION* (or equivalent) reflect the exchange.
** Outgoing instructions match the scenario (e.g. 3 funds out or 2 Static out).
** Incoming instructions match the scenario (e.g. 1 existing fund, 2 Static, or 1 Age-Based), with amounts consistent with the exchange.
* When *FUTUREALLOC=Y*, *TU_ACCT_ALLOC* (or equivalent) is validated as per framework.

The scenario is *accepted* when all of the following are true:

h3. 3. CSR Flow – Annual Exchange

* *user logs in* for the test case (CSR login succeeds).

* *user completes annual exchange:*
** *Change Investments* → *Annual Exchange Now* is opened.
** *Outgoing:* All balance from the account’s *2 funds* is taken out (by percent; effectively 100% from each / full redemption).
** *Incoming:* Amount is allocated across *3 funds* by percent; total = 100%.
** *Future allocation:* *Yes* is selected (FUTUREALLOC = Y).
** If the screen *"CSR: You must capture the caller information"* is displayed, *Transaction Channel* = *Primary Account Owner* is selected (TRANS_CHANNEL).
** *Submit* is clicked; transaction completes (no validation or system errors).
** *Done* is clicked after success.
* *Reason code:* Not required for this test case (REASON_CODE = N).

*** *Outgoing:* 2 instructions (one per fund), by percent, totalling 100%.
*** *Incoming:* 3 instructions (one per target fund), by percent, totalling 100%; amounts consistent with the exchange.
** *TU_ACCT_ALLOC* is updated to reflect the new allocation when *Future allocation = Yes* (validation query: {{member.transactions.investments.exchange.alloc.live}} vs temp).

* No unhandled assertion or application errors during the flow.

column1: IDP- change password from profile security section
column34:
Users should be able to change their account password from their profile’s security settings after they are already authenticated into the system. This flow is part of standard account maintenance and does not require any MFA challenge because the user is already logged in and verified.

The system must require the *current password*, enforce password policy rules, and securely update the password.
column573:
h3. *1. Access Path*

* User navigates to: *Profile → Security → Change Password*.
* “Change Password” option is visible only to authenticated users.

h3. *2. Form Requirements*

The Change Password form must contain:

* Current password
* New password
* Confirm new password

h3. *3. Validation*

* System verifies the current password is correct.
* New password must meet full password policy.
* New password must match the confirmation field.
* User cannot reuse the same current password (if policy disallows).

h3. *4. Password Update*

* Upon validation, system updates password successfully.
* User receives a success message and a confirmation notification (optional based on system settings).

column1: IDP- forgot password functionality
column34:
Users should be able to initiate a password change without logging in, via a “Change Password” link on the login screen. This flow must enforce Multi‑Factor Authentication (MFA) to ensure strong identity verification before allowing the user to set a new password.

This feature should include:

* “Change Password” option on the login screen
* MFA verification (SMS– based on system configuration)
* Secure token generation and validation
* New password creation flow with validation
column573:
*Password must be a minimum of 8 characters long and include 3 of the following: lower characters, upper characters, special characters, or numbers. Password cannot include first name, last name, username, or email.*

* Unit tests for MFA + token flow
* Integration tests for full password change process
* End‑to‑end test scenarios

#* Outgoing: one Age-Based fund selected with a percent (e.g. 100%).
#* Incoming: two Static funds selected with valid percents (totalling 100%).
# *Submission*
#* Transaction submits successfully (no validation or system errors).
#* Reason code is selected where required (e.g. 245 Letter of Indemnity per test data).
# Validation
#* *log and validate custom exchange* passes:
#** TU_FIN_TXN / TU_FIN_INSTRUCTION (or equivalent) reflect the exchange.
#** Outgoing instruction: 1 Age-Based fund, by Percent.
#** Incoming instructions: 2 Static funds, by Percent, amounts consistent with the exchange.
#* *CSR Reason Code Verification* passes for the test case.
# Data
#* Traunch has at least one eligible account with 1+ Age-Based fund (see {{docs/sql/find_traunch_for_exchange_2_age_based_to_2_static.sql}}; for this case the same query logic applies with 1 Age-Based fund).

column1: Custom Exchange 1 Fund to 2 Funds By Units to Percent
column573:
AC1 - Access & Navigation
Given CSR user is logged in and on member transactions, when user opens Custom Exchange, then exchange screen is available for fund-to-fund transaction entry.
AC2 - Eligible Fund Selection
Given account has eligible holdings, when user selects 1 source fund and 2 destination funds, then all selected funds are valid for exchange and source fund is different from both destination funds.
AC3 - Dollar-to-Dollar Mode
Given exchange type is Dollar to Dollar, when user enters source dollar amount and two destination dollar amounts, then system accepts valid currency format and positive values only.
AC4 - Amount Balance Rule
Given source and destination amounts are entered, when user submits, then source amount must equal the sum of destination 1 + destination 2 amounts.
AC5 - Source Balance Validation
Given source fund available balance is known, when entered source amount exceeds available/eligible balance, then submission is blocked with validation message.
AC6 - Required Field Validation
Given any mandatory field is missing (fund selection, amounts, reason code if required), when user submits, then transaction is not created and clear error is displayed.
AC7 - Restriction Validation
Given plan/fund restrictions exist (closed fund, blackout, frequency/round-trip rules), when violated, then transaction is blocked and rule-specific message is shown.
AC8 - Successful Submission
Given all validations pass, when user submits custom exchange, then confirmation is displayed with transaction details (account, source fund, destination funds, amounts, status/effective date).
AC9 - Transaction Integrity
Given transaction is accepted, when records are validated, then source fund decreases by submitted source amount and both destination funds increase by respective submitted destination amounts (with expected rounding rules).
AC10 - Reason Code & Audit
Given CSR completes submission, when audit/reason-code verification runs, then correct CSR reason code, user ID, timestamp, and transaction details are stored and traceable.
AC11 - Non-Partial Failure Handling
Given a system/backend error occurs during submit, when transaction fails, then no partial posting occurs and user receives actionable error message.

column1: Investment Options for Custom Exchange
column573:
AC1 - Access: Given a valid user with exchange permissions, when they open Custom Exchange, then the Transaction Investment Exchange section is visible and enabled.
AC2 - Eligible Funds: Given an account with available holdings, when user selects source/target funds, then only eligible exchange funds/plans are displayed.
AC3 - Amount/Percent Entry: Given user enters exchange instructions, when value is submitted, then system accepts only valid amount/percent formats and rejects invalid inputs.
AC4 - Balance Validation: Given source fund balance is known, when exchange is submitted, then entered amount does not exceed available/eligible balance.
AC5 - Minimum Rules: Given plan/fund minimum rules exist, when exchange is submitted, then minimum transaction and residual balance rules are enforced.
AC6 - Custom Allocation Total: Given multiple target funds are selected, when percentages are entered, then total target allocation must equal 100% (or configured total rule).
AC7 - Same Fund Prevention: Given source and destination are selected, when both are identical, then system blocks submission with clear error message.
AC8 - Trading Restrictions: Given fund/company restrictions (closed fund, blackout, exchange frequency, round-trip restrictions), when violated, then submission is blocked with rule-specific message.
AC9 - Cutoff/Trade Date: Given transaction submitted before/after cutoff, when confirmed, then trade date and effective timing follow configured business rules.
AC10 - Confirmation: Given submission succeeds, when user confirms, then a confirmation number and transaction summary are shown (source, target, amount/percent, date, status).
AC11 - Pending/Posted State: Given transaction is accepted, when viewed in history, then status flows correctly (Pending -> Posted/Completed) after nightly processing.
AC12 - Data Integrity: Given transaction posts, when balances are refreshed, then source decreases and target increases accurately with expected rounding.
AC13 - Audit Trail: Given any create/update/cancel event, when audit is reviewed, then user ID, timestamp, account, plan, transaction details, and status are logged.
AC14 - Error Handling: Given backend/service failure occurs, when submission is attempted, then transaction is not partially saved and user receives actionable error text.
AC15 - Regression Safety: Given existing exchange/contribution/withdrawal flows are in regression suite, when Custom Exchange is added, then existing scenarios continue to pass without behavioral change.

column1: Age based Annual Exchange
column573:
Eligible investment options are shown correctly
Only valid source funds (with available balance/units) are selectable.
Only valid destination funds are selectable based on plan/fund rules.
Ineligible, closed, or restricted funds are not selectable (or show clear reason).
Source and destination selection rules are enforced
A fund cannot be exchanged to itself unless explicitly allowed by business rules.
If multiple source funds are selected, each selected source must have sufficient amount/units.
Destination fund count and combinations must follow configured business limits.
Allocation method validations pass
For Dollar exchanges: amounts must be numeric, positive, and within available value.
For Percent exchanges: total destination allocation must equal exactly 100% (if full allocation required).
For Units exchanges: units must be valid precision and not exceed available units.
System blocks submit when totals mismatch or format/range is invalid.
Transaction submission and confirmation are correct
Exchange request saves successfully with a unique transaction/reference ID.
Confirmation screen/log shows correct source funds, destination funds, amounts/percents/units, and effective date.
Required CSR reason code is captured and persisted.
Post-transaction balances/holdings are updated accurately
Source holdings decrease by exchanged value/units as expected.
Destination holdings increase according to submitted allocation.
Rounding behavior follows business rules with no unexplained variance.
Audit trail and reporting are complete
Transaction appears in account history/activity with correct status.
Audit log records CSR user, timestamp, account, reason code, and transaction details.
Back-end/API/database validation (if part of test scope) matches UI result.

column1: Annual Exchange 2 Funds to 3 Funds
column573:
CSR can navigate to Annual Exchange from the member account (Change Investments → Annual Exchange Now).
The system displays the account’s current funds and allows the CSR to define 100% money-out from all existing funds (outgoing instructions).
The CSR can allocate 100% of the exchanged amount into one or more incoming funds by percentage, and the total incoming allocation equals 100%.
Incoming funds can be limited by type (e.g. Age-Based, Static) when specified in test data (INCOMING_FUND_TYPE / OUTGOING_FUND_TYPE).
The CSR can optionally set “Future Allocations” to Yes so future contributions use the new allocation (when supported by plan; e.g. not RIA).
When a reason code is required (REASON_CODE ≠ N), the CSR can select and submit a reason code.
For accounts over a defined threshold (e.g. balance > 50,000), the system may show a large-amount confirmation, and the CSR can acknowledge and continue.
Plan-specific prompts are handled (e.g. Advisor “Skip Class”, ABLE “caller information” / TRANS_CHANNEL).
After Submit, the transaction persisted and can be validated: TU_FIN_TXN and TU_FIN_INSTRUCTION reflect the outgoing and incoming instructions (by percent) for the account.
When future allocation is set (FUTUREALLOC=Y), allocation data is validated (e.g. temp vs live allocation tables).
Equity Wash Sale rule is enforced; if triggered, the test fails with a clear message.
Annual Exchange can be executed per plan/traunch (e.g. NYD, NYB, NYA, COA, MDD) with the correct feature and test data for that plan.

column573:
The Account Overview module from Stage1 must be included and executed as part of the Stage5 regression suite.
All test cases related to Account Overview in Stage1 must be ported and runnable in Stage5 without manual intervention.
The module must verify that account summary information (balances, recent transactions, account status, etc.) displays correctly for all supported account types in Stage5.
Navigation to the Account Overview page must work from all intended entry points (dashboard, menu, etc.) in Stage5.
All UI elements (labels, buttons, links, charts, etc.) present in Stage1 must be present and function identically in Stage5.
Data displayed in the Account Overview must match backend data for the test accounts in Stage5.
Any environment-specific configuration or data differences between Stage1 and Stage5 must be documented and accounted for in the tests.
The module must handle and report any errors or exceptions encountered during execution in Stage5.
Test results for the Account Overview module must be included in the overall Stage5 regression report, with clear pass/fail status for each test case.
Any failures or discrepancies must be logged, investigated, and resolved or documented as environment-specific issues.
The module should be compatible with automated execution (e.g., via CI/CD pipeline) in Stage5.
QA must review and sign off on the successful execution and coverage of the Account Overview module in Stage5.

column1: Regression Fix - Withdrawal module
column573:
The Withdrawal module must correctly process valid withdrawal requests for all supported account types and plans.
The system must validate all required fields (e.g., withdrawal amount, account selection, payment method) before allowing submission.
If any required field is missing or invalid, the user must receive a clear and actionable error message, and the withdrawal must not proceed.
The withdrawal amount must not exceed the available account balance or violate any plan-specific withdrawal limits.
The system must prevent duplicate withdrawal submissions (e.g., by disabling the submit button after the first click or by backend validation).
All withdrawal transactions must be accurately recorded in the backend/database, and transaction history must reflect the withdrawal with correct details.
The user must receive confirmation (on-screen and/or via email) upon successful withdrawal submission.
The module must handle and display errors gracefully for failed withdrawal attempts (e.g., backend errors, insufficient funds, invalid account status).
Automated regression tests must cover positive and negative withdrawal scenarios, including edge cases (e.g., minimum/maximum limits, invalid input, session timeout).
The fix must be verified across all supported browsers and devices.
No regressions should be introduced in related modules (e.g., account overview, transaction history) as a result of the fix.
QA must review and sign off on the fix, confirming that all withdrawal scenarios are handled as expected and that the module meets business and technical requirements.

column1: Regression Fix - Enrollment Negative (Routing number issue) for PAB
column573:
The system must validate the routing number field during enrollment and reject any invalid or incorrectly formatted routing numbers.
When a user enters an invalid routing number, an appropriate error message must be displayed, clearly indicating the issue (e.g., "Please enter a valid routing number.").
The enrollment process must not proceed to the next step if the routing number is invalid.
The error message must be visible and accessible to all users, including those using assistive technologies.
The routing number validation must cover:
Incorrect length (not 9 digits)
Non-numeric characters
Known invalid routing numbers (e.g., failing checksum/modulus validation)
The system must log all failed enrollment attempts due to routing number issues for audit and troubleshooting purposes.
Automated regression tests must be updated or added to cover negative scenarios for routing number validation, ensuring that invalid routing numbers are consistently rejected.
The fix must be verified across all supported browsers and devices.
No valid routing numbers should be incorrectly rejected.
QA must review and sign off on the fix, confirming that all negative routing number scenarios are handled as expected and that no regressions are introduced elsewhere in the enrollment flow.

column1: Swagger API Project - Create endpoint testing & validation project
column34:
Goal

* Add a new Maven module named: swagger-api-smoke (or similar) under the repo.

* It must be simple, modular, configurable, and reusable.

* It will validate that Swagger JSON endpoints return HTTP 200 and contain a top-level field "openapi".

* The test should cover these endpoints by default:

# [https://agsup-aws-account-web-qc4.gs.ascensus.com/accounts/swagger-api|https://agsup-aws-account-web-qc4.gs.ascensus.com/accounts/swagger-api]

# [https://agsup-validation-web-qc4.gs.ascensus.com/validation/swagger-api|https://agsup-validation-web-qc4.gs.ascensus.com/validation/swagger-api]

# [https://agsup-metadata-web-qc4.gs.ascensus.com/metadata/swagger-api|https://agsup-metadata-web-qc4.gs.ascensus.com/metadata/swagger-api]



Validation rules

* Assert status code == 200

* Assert Content-Type contains "application/json" (don’t be overly strict; accept charset)

* Assert JSON has key "openapi" at the root and it is not blank

* Optionally assert openapi matches regex: ^3\\.\\d+\\.\\d+.* (make it configurable)

* If the response is not JSON or parsing fails, fail with a clear error message including endpoint and first ~300 chars of body



Configurability

* Endpoints must NOT be hardcoded only in test code.

* Provide a default config file (src/test/resources/swagger-endpoints.yaml or .properties) listing endpoints.

* Allow overriding via env var SWAGGER_ENDPOINTS (comma-separated URLs). If present, use it instead of file.

* Allow setting timeout via env var SWAGGER_TIMEOUT_MS (default 10000).

* Allow optional header injection via env vars:

* SWAGGER_AUTH_HEADER (e.g., "Authorization")

* SWAGGER_AUTH_VALUE (e.g., "Bearer xxx")

If both present, add that header to all requests. Otherwise no auth.



Implementation requirements

* Use JUnit 5 with a parameterized test (one test per endpoint).

* Use RestAssured for the HTTP call.

* Use JsonPath or Jackson to parse and verify openapi field.

* Use a small helper class to load endpoints and settings (e.g., SwaggerConfig / EndpointProvider).

* Use a small client/wrapper class (e.g., SwaggerApiClient) to execute the GET and return response details.

* Keep dependencies minimal: rest-assured, junit-jupiter, jackson-databind (if needed), snakeyaml (only if yaml used). Prefer .properties to avoid adding snakeyaml unless you really want yaml.

column1: Prefill Enrollment – "This functionality is not available at this time" after completing bank details / Save and Send (Stage 1, 02/18/2026)  Description
column34:
Prefill Enrollment regression failed on 02/18/2026. After completing the bank details section and clicking *Save and Send*, the application displays: "This functionality is not available at this time. Please try again later." (with a changing timestamp). The issue prevents users from proceeding and is consistently reproducible. *Plans affected:* WFD, PAD, VGI, NUV.

*Environment*
Stage 1. Test: Prime Test Automation V3 – PrefillEnrollmentPositive.feature (frontoffice/csr/prefillenroll).

*Observed behavior*

* User completes bank details and clicks Save and Send.
* Error message appears at the top of the page immediately after submit.
* Message includes a timestamp (e.g. ts2 = 02/18/2026 13:13:30) that changes with each attempt.
* User cannot proceed to the next step; enrollment is blocked.

*Expected behavior*
User should be able to proceed to the next step after entering valid bank details; no error message should be displayed.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Start Prefill Enrollment and complete all sections up to bank details.
# Enter valid bank details and click Save and Send.
# Observe the error message at the top of the page.

h3. Expected Result

User proceeds to the next step after valid bank details; no error.

h3. Actual Result

Error: "This functionality is not available at this time. Please try again later." with changing timestamp; user cannot proceed.

h3. Environment

* *Environment:* Stage 1
* *Date:* 02/18/2026
* *Test suite:* Prefill Enrollment – PrefillEnrollmentPositive.feature
* *Path:* frontoffice/csr/prefillenroll/feature/PrefillEnrollmentPositive.feature
* *Plans affected:* WFD, PAD, VGI, NUV

h3. Priority / Severity

* *Severity:* Major (S3) – Core CSR prefill enrollment path broken at bank details step.

column1: Regression Fix -Enrollment Negative Validation Issue ABLE contribution Limit for PAB
column573:
If a user attempts to enroll or make a contribution that exceeds the annual ABLE contribution limit, the system must display a clear and accurate error message indicating the contribution limit has been exceeded.
The error message must be consistent with the requirements and wording defined in the business rules and user documentation.
The system must prevent the user from proceeding with the enrollment or contribution if the limit is exceeded.
The validation must be enforced for all ABLE plans and for all contribution types (AIP, EBT, Mail, etc.).
The validation must work for both single and multiple account enrollments/contributions.
The error must be logged appropriately for audit and troubleshooting purposes.
Automated regression tests must verify that the error is triggered when the contribution limit is exceeded and not triggered when within the limit.
The validation must be effective in all supported environments (local, QA, staging, production).
No false positives or negatives: users within the contribution limit must not see the error, and users exceeding the limit must always see the error.
The fix must not impact other enrollment or contribution flows for ABLE or non-ABLE plans.

All waits and element locators must be robust against minor UI changes (e.g., dynamic IDs, delayed rendering).
The fix must be validated for the MID plan across all required user roles in the regression suite.

column1: IDP Forgot Username redirects to error page instead of recovery flow – Stage 1 (02/11/2026)
column34:
IDP Forgot Username regression failed on 02/11/2026. When the user clicks "Forgot Username" on the IDP login page, the application redirects to an error page ({{/app/error/tmB5tn.html}}) instead of the username recovery page. The error page shows "The information you are looking for regarding your NJBEST 529 College Savings Plan Account is temporarily unavailable…" Automation then fails with TimeoutException looking for the Email address input, which is only present on the recovery page.

*Environment*
Stage 1 ([njd.stage1.acs529.com|http://njd.stage1.acs529.com]). Test: Prime Test Automation V3 – IDPForgotUsernameUnregisteredNegative.feature.

*Observed behavior*

* User clicks "Forgot Username" on IDP login page.
* Application redirects to {{https://njd.stage1.acs529.com/app/error/tmB5tn.html}}.
* Error page displays temporary-unavailable message; no recovery form (e.g. Email address field) is shown.
* Dev tools: "Failed to load response data – No content available because this request was redirected."
* Automation fails: TimeoutException / NoSuchElementException for {{auth-form-input[label='Email address']}}.

*Expected behavior*
User should be redirected to the appropriate Forgot Username recovery page with Email address (and/or phone) input, where they can enter unregistered email/phone and see "No account found with this information."

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Go to IDP login: [https://njd.stage1.acs529.com/njdtpl/auth/ll.cs|https://njd.stage1.acs529.com/njdtpl/auth/ll.cs]
# Click "Forgot Username."
# Observe redirect to error page instead of recovery page.

h3. Expected Result

User is redirected to the Forgot Username recovery page; Email address (or equivalent) input is visible.

h3. Actual Result

Redirect to {{/app/error/tmB5tn.html}} with "temporarily unavailable" message; recovery form not displayed; automation fails with TimeoutException.

h3. Environment

* *Environment:* Stage 1
* *URL (login):* [https://njd.stage1.acs529.com/njdtpl/auth/ll.cs|https://njd.stage1.acs529.com/njdtpl/auth/ll.cs]

column1: Universal Enrollment fails at review with BadSqlGrammarException – UP_SEMANTIC_VALIDATION.ENROLLMENT_529_VALIDATE_GET parameters not fully passed from frontend (Stage 1, 02/03/2026)
column34:
Universal Enrollment regression failed on 02/03/2026. At the enrollment review step, semantic validation fails with *BadSqlGrammarException*. The callable statement {{UP_SEMANTIC_VALIDATION.ENROLLMENT_529_VALIDATE_GET(?, ?, ?)}} is invoked but not all parameters are being fed/filled correctly from the frontend, causing bad SQL grammar and blocking enrollment completion.

*Environment*
Stage 1 (e.g., [njd.stage1.acs529.com|http://njd.stage1.acs529.com]). Test: Prime Test Automation V3 – unite-universal-enrollment.

*Observed behavior*

* User reaches review page: "You're almost there! – Review and submit your information."
* On submit/validation, red error banner displays BadSqlGrammarException (see screenshot).
* Network tab (validation request): response contains same exception; {{field}}: SemanticValidator, {{objectName}}: EnrollmentRequestDto.
* Enrollment does not complete; automation then fails with "Database results are empty" when validating DB.

*Technical detail*

* Stored procedure: {{UP_SEMANTIC_VALIDATION.ENROLLMENT_529_VALIDATE_GET(?, ?, ?)}} (3 parameters).
* Issue: Parameter binding/count or values from frontend to backend not correct – "not feeding or filling all the parameters."

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Go to Universal Enrollment (e.g., NJ Direct) and complete all steps to review page.
# On review page, trigger submit/review (proceed or submit).
# Observe error on page and in validation API response (Network tab).

h3. Expected Result

Semantic validation runs successfully; enrollment completes; no BadSqlGrammarException.

h3. Actual Result

BadSqlGrammarException on {{UP_SEMANTIC_VALIDATION.ENROLLMENT_529_VALIDATE_GET(?, ?, ?)}}; enrollment does not complete; automation fails with "Database results are empty."

h3. Environment

* *Environment:* Stage 1
* *URL (example):* [njd.stage1.acs529.com/enrollments/njdirect/review|http://njd.stage1.acs529.com/enrollments/njdirect/review]

column1: Exception occurs during Next Gen enrollment submission when enrolling multiple accounts with identical beneficiary information (MID/WID)
column34:
*Issue Overview:* During Next Gen enrollment for MID/WID (Multiple Individual Designation/Witness Individual Designation) accounts, the system throws an exception when attempting to submit enrollment for multiple accounts that share the same beneficiary information. This prevents successful completion of multi-beneficiary enrollment workflows.

*Context:*

* Feature: Next Gen Enrollment System - Multi-Beneficiary Enrollment
* Component: Enrollment Submission Module
* Test Environment: [ENVIRONMENT_NAME]
* Discovered: January 26, 2026
* Reproducibility: 100% - Confirmed reproducible manually by Pallavi Nookala

*Technical Details:* The failure occurs specifically when:

* Two or more accounts are enrolled in the same enrollment session
* Both accounts designate the same beneficiary with identical identifying information:
** SSN: 741258963
** Date of Birth: 02/18/2024
* The exception is triggered at the point of enrollment submission

*Business Impact:*

* Blocks multi-beneficiary enrollment scenarios where the same beneficiary is designated across multiple accounts
* Prevents users from completing enrollment workflows for families or multiple account holders with shared beneficiaries
* May impact enrollment completion rates and user experience

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Navigate to Next Gen enrollment portal for MID/WID enrollment type
# Begin enrollment process for Account 01 (AO: Account Owner)
# Enter beneficiary information for Account 01:
#* Beneficiary SSN: 741258963
#* Beneficiary Date of Birth: 02/18/2024
#* Complete all required beneficiary fields
# Add Account 02 to the same enrollment session
# Enter beneficiary information for Account 02 using the *same beneficiary details*:
#* Beneficiary SSN: 741258963 (same as Account 01)
#* Beneficiary Date of Birth: 02/18/2024 (same as Account 01)
#* Complete all required beneficiary fields
# Complete all enrollment steps for both accounts (fund selection, contribution amounts, banking information, etc.)
# Proceed to enrollment submission/review page
# Click "Submit Enrollment" or equivalent submission button
# *Observe:* System throws an exception instead of processing the enrollment

h3. Expected Result

The system should successfully process and submit the enrollment for both accounts, even when they share the same beneficiary information. The enrollment should:

* Validate all account and beneficiary information
* Process both accounts through the enrollment workflow
* Generate enrollment confirmations for both accounts
* Complete the enrollment submission without errors
* Allow the same beneficiary to be designated across multiple accounts in a single enrollment session

h3. Actual Result

*Observed Behavior:*

* System throws an exception at the point of enrollment submission
* Enrollment submission fails and does not complete
* No enrollment confirmation is generated for either account
* Error message/exception is displayed (see attached screenshot: {{Nextgen Multi Bene enrollment error.png}})

*Exception Details:*

* Exception occurs during enrollment submission processing
* Full exception details available in failure report: {{Nextgen Multiple Bene Enrollment Failure Report.docx}}
* Error prevents completion of the enrollment workflow

*Reproducibility:*

* *Status:* 100% Reproducible
* *Confirmed By:* Pallavi Nookala (Manual Reproduction)
* *Test Environment:* [ENVIRONMENT_NAME]
* *Date Confirmed:* January 26, 2026

h3. Environment

* *Environment:* [ENVIRONMENT_NAME - e.g., QA, Stage1, Stage2]
* *Application Version:* [VERSION_NUMBER]
* *Browser:* [BROWSER_NAME and VERSION - if applicable]
* *Date/Time Discovered:* January 26, 2026
* *Test Type:* Regression Testing / Manual Testing
* *Test Execution:* Daily Regression - TestNG Report 01262026

column1: Stage 1 | Make Contribution fails for Direct Plans with JSPException – Nightly Regression
column34:
The *Make Contribution* feature is consistently failing in *nightly regression* for *Direct plans* in Stage 1.

All tested Direct plans return the same JSP-level error during contribution flow.
This issue has been observed *consistently across the last nightly regression* and is not transient.

----

h3. *Observed Behavior*

On initiating Make Contribution, the application fails with:

{quote}*JSPException including path* {{/cs529/txn/contribType.jsp}}
_“This functionality is not available at this time. Please try again later.”_{quote}

The failure occurs before contribution type selection is completed.

----

h3. *Expected Behavior*

Users should be able to successfully access the contribution flow and complete contributions for Direct plans.

----

h3. *Scope / Impact*

* ❌ *Direct plans:* Failing consistently
* ✅ *ABLE plans:* Working as expected (AKB, NYB validated)
* ❌ *PAGSP:* Also failing
* ✅ *Legacy system:* Contribution flows look good

This indicates the issue is *isolated to Direct-plan contribution flow*, not ABLE or legacy paths.

----

h3. *Automation Evidence*

* Observed in *last nightly regression*
* Reproduced across multiple Direct plans
* Screenshots and account numbers captured separately

----

h3. *Notes*

* Issue is *not related to current development changes*
* Behavior is consistent and reproducible
* Requires tracking as a functional defect
column724:
A recent code change related to contribution link/property handling (introduced during Umbra migration updates) caused incorrect resolution of the contribution URL at runtime for Direct plans. This resulted in the JSP failing to load the contribution type page.

*Fix Implemented*

* The contribution property handling was corrected.
* The fix was deployed to Stage 1.
* Application servers were cycled to ensure the updated configuration was fully applied.

*Validation*
QA manually verified Make Contribution in Stage 1 for the following plans:

* AKB 
* MID 
* NJD 
* NMD 
* NYB 
* NYD

All plans are now working as intended with no errors observed.

column1: Regression Fix - Enrollment Negative (Contribution Limit Exceed) for EBT
column573:
When a user enters an EBT (Electronic Bank Transfer) contribution amount that exceeds the allowed limit during enrollment, the system must display a clear error message (e.g., Amount cannot be more than $20,000.00.).
The enrollment process must block submission if the EBT amount exceeds the configured maximum.
The error message must appear immediately after the user enters the invalid amount (on blur or form submission).
The validation must use the correct EBT limit as defined in the plan configuration or resource file (e.g., funding.able.max.amount.ebt).
The error message must match the text in the resource file for consistency.
The fix must not impact successful enrollments where the EBT amount is within the allowed limit.
Automated regression tests must verify that exceeding the EBT limit blocks enrollment and displays the correct error message.
All validation errors must be logged for audit purposes.

column1: Regression Fix - Enrollment Negative (Contribution Limit Exceed)
column573:
If a user attempts to contribute an amount exceeding the annual or one-time contribution limit during enrollment, the system must display a clear error message (e.g., "Amount cannot be more than $20,000.00.").
The enrollment process must not proceed or allow submission when the contribution amount exceeds the defined limit.
The error message must be shown immediately after the user enters an invalid amount, either on blur or on form submission.
The contribution amount field must be validated for both annual and one-time limits as per the plan rules.
The system must log the validation error for audit purposes.
The fix must not impact successful enrollments where the contribution amount is within the allowed limits.
Automated regression tests must verify that exceeding the limit blocks enrollment and displays the correct error message.
The error message must be consistent with the text defined in the resource file (e.g., funding.able.max.amount.aip and funding.able.max.amount.ebt).

column1: Stage1 | Enrollment pages fail to load at initial step (Browser/URL issue) – Multiple plans excluding NYD
column34:
Multiple enrollment scenarios in Stage 1 are failing during automation regression due to *failure at the initial enrollment step*, primarily when loading the enrollment page in the browser.

Most failures occur *before meaningful user interaction*, indicating a *URL routing / application availability issue*, not data or validation failures.

This impacts multiple enrollment flows (NextGen, Legacy, CSR) across non-NYD plans.

----

h3. *Observed Behavior*

* Enrollment scenarios fail at or immediately after browser launch / page load
* Page does not load successfully or errors out early
* Downstream steps are skipped as a result

Failures are clustered around:

* Initial enrollment URL access
* Early page load / initialization

----

h3. *Expected Behavior*

Enrollment pages should load successfully in the browser and allow users to proceed with the enrollment flow.

----

h3. *Automation Evidence*

*Regression Suite:*
Regression Test Suite – Enrollments
{{stage1-enrollments.xml}}

*Summary:*

* Total Tests: 25
* Methods: 110
* Failed: 51
* Passed: 59

*Execution Report:*
[http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20260115/stage1-enrollments/#|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20260115/stage1-enrollments/#]

----

h3. *Failure Pattern (Important)*

* NYD failures observed but *intentionally ignored*
* Save-as-Draft failures *intentionally excluded*
* Remaining failures consistently occur *early in the flow*
* Strong indicator of *environment / routing / application readiness issue*

----

h3. *Repro Steps (Representative)*

# Launch enrollment flow for a non-NYD plan
# Browser opens enrollment URL
# Enrollment page fails to load or errors immediately

----

h3. *Impact / Risk*

* Member enrollment blocked across multiple plans
* Regression confidence compromised
* Risk to downstream testing and release readiness if not addressed

----

h3. *Notes*

* Failures are *systemic*, not isolated
* Not related to test data
* Not related to Save-as-Draft known defects
* NYD explicitly excluded due to migration to IDPIN Universal Enrollment
column724:
The early morning failures (~4 AM) coincided with an active deployment window, causing temporary environment instability.
A later automated pipeline build (~6 AM) stabilized the environment and appears to have resolved both issues.

We’ve revalidated the impacted scenarios locally and manually, and things are working as expected now.
We’ll continue to monitor and confirm via subsequent runs.

column1: Stage1 | NextGen Enrollment fails at Submit – “Functionality not available” across MID & NYD plans
column34:
Multiple NextGen Enrollment scenarios are consistently failing in Stage 1 during automation regression at the *Submit / Finalization* step.

The application displays a functional error preventing enrollment completion:

{quote}*“Your request cannot be completed at this time. Please contact Plan administrator.”*
*ErrorCode:* _This functionality is not available at this time. Please try again later._{quote}

This impacts *most MID and NYD NextGen enrollment flows*, including payroll, check, AIP, trusted contact, and multiple-beneficiary scenarios.

----

h3. *Observed Behavior*

* Enrollment submission fails with system-level error
* No successful completion for affected plans
* Failures are *consistent across multiple scenarios and features*

h3. *Expected Behavior*

* Enrollment submission should complete successfully for valid NextGen plans in Stage 1

----

h3. *Automation Evidence*

* *Regression Suite:* Regression Test Suite – Enrollments
* *Runner:* stage1-enrollments.xml
* *Total Tests:* 41
* *Failed:* 31
* *Passed:* 10

*Report Link*
[http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20260106/stage1-enrollments/#|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20260106/stage1-enrollments/#]

*Exception Logs*

* [http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20260106/stage1-enrollments/mid.20260106034342195.T100_NextGenEnrollmentPositive.feature.178_exception_failedresult.txt|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20260106/stage1-enrollments/mid.20260106034342195.T100_NextGenEnrollmentPositive.feature.178_exception_failedresult.txt]
* [http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20260106/stage1-enrollments/mid.20260106034355682.T100_NextGenEnrollmentPositive.feature.138_exception_failedresult.txt|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20260106/stage1-enrollments/mid.20260106034355682.T100_NextGenEnrollmentPositive.feature.138_exception_failedresult.txt]

----

h3. *Impacted Scenarios (Sample)*

* MID & NYD NextGen Enrollment – Payroll / Check / AIP
* Trusted Contact flows
* Multiple Beneficiary enrollments
* Various FUND IDs (e.g., 5472001, 1003014)

----

h3. *Repro Steps*

# Launch NextGen Enrollment (MID or NYD plan)
# Complete enrollment details with valid data
# Submit enrollment
# System returns “Functionality not available” error

----

h3. *Risk / Impact*

* Core enrollment workflows blocked in Stage 1
* Automation regression unusable for enrollment confidence
* Potential release readiness risk if present beyond Stage 1

----

h3. *Notes*

* Failures are *not flaky*
* Same error observed across multiple features and runs
* Indicates backend/service/config issue, not test instability

column1: Stage 1 | NYD Client | Application fails on account entry – JSP header exception blocks all flows
column34:
A *Stage 1–specific failure is occurring for the NYD client*, where the application errors out immediately after entering an NYD account number.
The issue results in a JSP header exception and prevents *all NYD-related functionality* from proceeding.

Other plans appear to be unaffected at this time.

----

h3. *Steps to Reproduce*

# Navigate to Prime application in *Stage 1*
# Select *NYD* client / plan
# Enter a valid NYD account number
# Submit / proceed

----

h3. *Actual Result*

Application fails immediately after account entry with a generic error message.
Observed error:

{noformat}Error: please try again later.
java.io.IOException: JSPException including path '/cs529/default/header.jsp'
ErrorCode: This functionality is not available at this time.
{noformat}

User is blocked from proceeding further.
All NYD flows (including contributions and related features) fail.

----

h3. *Expected Result*

NYD account should load successfully and allow the user to proceed with contributions and other supported NYD flows without error.

----

h3. *Frequency*

100% reproducible for NYD in Stage 1
First observed *today (early morning)*

----

h3. *Impact / Risk*

* NYD client is *fully blocked* in Stage 1
* Impacts *all NYD features*, not limited to a single flow
* High risk for validation, downstream testing, and release readiness if not resolved quickly

----

h3. *Initial Triage Notes (QA Observation)*

* Error originates from {{/cs529/default/header.jsp}}
* Appears *client-specific (NYD only)*
* Likely related to *configuration, mount, or JSP dependency* rather than feature logic
* Other plans do not show the same behavior

----

h3. *Attachments / References*

* *Automation report:*
[http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20260105/stage1-contributions/|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20260105/stage1-contributions/]

column1: Dynamic Test Data & Environment Decoupling (Metadata APIs)

Implement dynamic test data strategy to eliminate tight coupling to static environment data for Metadata API automation.

----

h3. *Description*

Current Metadata API tests rely on static accounts and fixed data, making them fragile after DB refreshes. This story introduces a dynamic and resilient test data strategy to ensure long-term stability and environment independence.

----

h3. *Scope / Tasks*

* Identify all static data dependencies in Metadata tests
* Define dynamic data selection strategy (derived/query-based/rule-based)
* Refactor tests to consume dynamic data
* Validate behavior post DB refresh
* Update documentation to reflect new data strategy
column573:

* No hardcoded environment-specific accounts

column1: Metadata API Phase-1 Baseline Stabilization
column34:
Stabilize existing Metadata API automation suite and prepare it for CI/CD baseline execution.

----

h3. *Description*

The Metadata API automation suite currently exists but is tightly coupled to static environment data and partially disabled scenarios. This story focuses on stabilizing the existing test suite so it can run deterministically across environments (QC4 and Stage1) and serve as a trusted Phase-1 regression baseline.

----

h3. *Scope / Tasks*

* Analyze existing Metadata API tests and failures
* Remove hard dependency on static environment-specific accounts
* Isolate or refactor MAP/login-dependent scenarios
* Ensure tests are resilient to DB refreshes
* Identify and document disabled tests and gaps
* Validate local execution using Maven profiles
* Align test behavior with documented validation strategy

----

h3. *Out of Scope*

* Coverage expansion
* New scenario creation
* Pipeline creation (handled separately)
column573:

* Disabled tests are explicitly documented
* Tests are resilient to DB refresh
* Suite is ready for CI/CD execution
* QC4 and Stage1 can be targeted via configuration

column1: Scrum Of Scrum - Analyze & Define Approach for API Testing Integration into CI/CD Pipeline
column34:
Scrum Of Scrum requires a fully integrated *API testing pipeline* that executes authentication and PXE flow validations as part of CI/CD. This spike will investigate all technical, architectural, and configuration requirements needed to implement automated API tests (Postman/RestAssured) into the existing pipeline.

The outcome of this spike is to provide *clear implementation requirements*, *technical decisions*, *scope boundaries*, and *a ready plan* for development stories.

h1. *Objectives / What This Spike Must Answer*

h3. *1. Identify API Test Scope for Initial Integration*

* Authentication API validations
* PXE flow API calls (end-to-end request/response validation)
* Client ID creation & verification
* Token generation + token validation
* Error handling & negative test scenarios
* Environment separation requirements (Stage 1 / QA / Local)

h3. *2. Evaluate Existing API Test Assets*

* Review current Postman collections (folder structure, variables, test scripts).
* Identify if RestAssured or other Java-based frameworks already exist.
* Check if test data references or secrets handling patterns are available.
* Confirm authentication mechanisms (OAuth2, Client ID/Secret, JWT, MSAL, etc.).

h3. *3. Determine CI/CD Pipeline Integration Requirements*

* Identify required package layout in GitLab ({{api-test-automation}} module).
* Confirm how artifacts will be published into Nexus.
* Define GitLab job structure:
** Install dependencies
** Run API tests
** Export test results (JUnit / HTML)
** Upload artifacts and evidence
* Ensure compatibility with existing QA Automation pipelines.

h3. *4. Identify API Environment Configuration Needs*

* Environment variables required (BASE URLs, CLIENT_ID, TENANT, SCOPES, SECRETS).
* Secure storage approach (GitLab CI variables, Vault, Secrets Manager).
* Request/response logging strategy.
* Retry logic & stability considerations for external services.

h3. *5. Local Execution Setup*

* Ability to run API tests locally via:
** Newman CLI OR
** RestAssured Maven/TestNG suite
* Validate that tests pass locally before pipeline integration.
* Document setup for onboarding new team members.

h3. *6. Knowledge Transfer and Dependencies*

* KT with ScrumOps owner(s) for API documentation & Postman environment files.
* Confirm versions and endpoints with Dev/Architecture teams.
* Identify dependencies such as:
** Access to environments (tokens, secrets)
** Firewall/Networking controls
** Missing documentation or unclear flows

h1. *Deliverables (Outcome of This Spike)*

At the end of this spike, the team will deliver:

h3. ✔ *Technical Findings Document*

Including:

* Recommended framework (Postman + Newman or Java + RestAssured)
* Required configs and variables
* Pipeline structure (YAML example)
* Gaps & dependencies
* Risks & mitigations

h3. ✔ *Local working prototype*

Proof-of-concept execution demonstrating:

* Successful authentication
* At least one PXE flow API call end-to-end
* Token validation test

h3. ✔ *Checklist for Implementation Stories*

* Code scaffolding
* Directory structure
* Postman collection cleanup (if needed)
* CI/CD YAML configuration
* Logging & reporting strategy
* Nexus artifact deployment

h3. ✔ *Refined Estimates for Follow-up Stories*

Two follow-up stories will be refined after spike completion:

# Build & configure API automation framework
# Integrate API tests into GitLab pipeline (end‑to‑end)

h1. *Notes*

* This spike does *not* implement production-ready pipeline code.
* Intended outcome is clarity and readiness for execution.
column573:
Spike is considered complete when:

* All required information for architecture & pipeline integration is documented.
* Local execution for API test flow is validated and reproducible.
* Clear recommendations and next steps are provided.
* All dependencies (DevOps, Secrets, Env Access) are identified.
* Follow‑up Jira stories can be created/refined with accurate scope & ACs.

column573:

The system verifies if the user has an existing ABLE account using provided credentials (e.g., account number, SSN, or email).
If the account exists, the user is prompted to re-register by creating new login credentials (username, password, security questions).
Password and security requirements are enforced during re-registration.
Upon successful re-registration, the user receives a confirmation and can log in with the new credentials.
If the account does not exist or credentials are invalid, an appropriate error message is displayed.
All re-registration activities are logged for audit purposes.
The process complies with accessibility and security standards.
The user can contact support if issues occur during re-registration.
Duplicate registrations for the same account are prevented.

column1: [DAILY] Automation Regression Health Check (V2 & V3) – 7:30 AM Triage & Reporting - Nov Week 2 (12/8-12/12)
column34:
Every business day at *7:30 AM* (local to offshore), perform a *daily health check* of the *Prime V2* and *Prime V3* regression jobs that finished overnight.
Tasks include: review *qTest* executions and *TestNG* reports, scan failures *by module*, reproduce locally or run a focused re-execution to confirm legitimacy, and communicate results.

* *If all green:* post a short status update to the team channel.
* *If failures:* email the Project Lead (Swapnil) following the bug-handling guidelines with the required evidence (plan, module, test case(s), exception, logs/screenshots, links). Swapnil will open/route defects.

*Goal:* Provide a reliable, single source of truth each morning on regression health (V2 + V3), with actionable failure details.

*Linked Docs:*

* *Bug Handling Process:* [https://confluence.ascensus.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=326751557|https://confluence.ascensus.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=326751557]
* *TestNG (V2) Report Index:* [http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/?C=M;O=D|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/?C=M;O=D]
* *V3 Pipelines (TestNG inside job artifacts):* [https://gitlab.com/ascensus-gs/products/depot/qa-automation/prime-test-automation/-/pipeline_schedules|https://gitlab.com/ascensus-gs/products/depot/qa-automation/prime-test-automation/-/pipeline_schedules]
* *qTest Portal (Project 90985):* [https://ascensus.qtestnet.com/p/90985/portal/project#tab=testexecution|https://ascensus.qtestnet.com/p/90985/portal/project#tab=testexecution]

column1: Prime V3 – Universal Enrollment: “Next” button unresponsive on Beneficiary Address step (Stage 1 | MDD/NJD/AKD)
column34:
*Summary:*
*Prime V3 – Universal Enrollment: “Next” button unresponsive on Beneficiary Address step (Stage 1 | MDD/NJD/AKD)*

*Environment:*
Stage 1 – Universal Enrollment (UE) Prime V3

*Plans Impacted:* MDD, NJD, AKD (confirmed), MD plan also reported by Valerie.

*Description:*
Automation and manual testers are blocked at the Beneficiary Address step in Universal Enrollment.
After completing all required fields and selecting *Yes/No* for “Does the future scholar have the same mailing address as you?”, clicking *Next* does nothing.
No validation messages appear, and the page does not advance.

This is *NOT automation flakiness*. Issue is reproduced manually & via multiple plans.

----

h3. *Steps to Reproduce*

# Navigate to Stage 1 Universal Enrollment
Example: {{https://mdd.stage1.acs529.com/enrollments/...}}
# Proceed through Owner → Beneficiary information
# At “Does the future scholar have the same mailing address as you?” select Yes/No
# Click *Next*
# Page freezes → no navigation, no logs indicating progression

----

h3. *Expected Result*

User should proceed to the next enrollment page.

h3. *Actual Result*

Page remains stuck — *Next* button does not respond.

----

*Automation Report:*

column1: Performance Testing – Framework Ownership, Environment Setup, CI/CD, Governance & Regression Integration
column34:
Performance Testing – Framework Ownership, Environment Setup, CI/CD, Governance & Regression Integration

h3. *Epic Goal:*

Enable Priti to *fully own*, *execute*, *maintain*, and *scale* the performance testing function at Ascensus, including framework setup, environment readiness, pipeline integration, documentation, and governance.

This includes taking over *existing performance projects*, *IDP deadlock detection suites*, *Taurus/JMeter/BlazeMeter frameworks*, and creating a *repeatable, governed, CI/CD-driven performance testing process*.

----

This EPIC captures all work required for onboarding, enabling, and transitioning performance testing responsibilities, including:

h3. *1. Environment Setup & Access*

* Local setup for *Taurus*, *JMeter*, *BlazeMeter*, *k6 if needed*, *Java*, *Python 3*, *Homebrew/Chocolatey*, and *GitLab CLI*
* Access to:
** GitLab Performance Repos
🔗 [https://gitlab.com/ascensus-gs/products/depot/qa-automation/automation/-/tree/main/performance-test-automation|https://gitlab.com/ascensus-gs/products/depot/qa-automation/automation/-/tree/main/performance-test-automation]
** Jenkins Performance Jobs
** Stage1/Stage2 API credentials
** Performance Test Data
** New Relic Dashboards
** Splunk Logs
** IDP Infrastructure Access (read-only)

----

h3. *2. Knowledge Transfer (KT) From Nick*

* IDP Deadlock Detection Framework
* MF3 / PolyLinux infrastructure
* Performance test execution patterns
* Test data requirements
* Jenkins integration
* Interpreting performance metrics
* Handling memory leak / CPU spike / deadlock alerts

----

h3. *3. Framework Ownership*

Priti will take ownership of:

* *IDP Deadlock Test Suite*
* *CSR Load Test*
* *UE Enrollment Performance Test*
* *Systematic Withdrawal Performance Test*
* *GSP/Partner Load Tests*
* *Account Summary burst test*
* *Login load tests (CSR + Member + IDP)*

This includes:

* Updating scripts
* Modularizing Taurus YAML files
* Improving data-driven execution
* Adding authentication token refresh logic
* Standardizing the repo folder structure
* Increasing readability & maintainability

----

h3. *4. Local Execution Capability*

Priti must be able to:

* Run any test locally
* Debug Taurus & JMeter failures
* Validate data setup
* Profile performance bottlenecks using logs
* Analyze CSV & HTML reports

----

h3. *5. CI/CD Integration*

Build proper CI/CD for performance testing:

* Jenkins job creation (nightly or on-demand)
* GitLab pipeline for performance suites
* Ability to trigger performance tests on merge requests
* Automated thresholds (fail build if throughput < X or latency > Y)

----

h3. *6. Regression Performance Package*

Create a *minimal but stable performance regression suite* that runs:

* IDP deadlock check
* Login throughput test
* Account summary stress test
* Contribution/Withdrawal basic load test
* UE Enrollment load test (lightweight)

These MUST run:

* *Nightly*
* Post-deployment
* Before major releases

----

h3. *7. Reporting & Observability*

Ownership of:

* Splunk dashboards → performance logs
* New Relic → CPU, Memory, Apdex, DB calls
* Performance Test HTML & CSV reports
* Execution trend tracking (historical baseline)

Deliverables:

* Upload results to Confluence per run
* Alert squad when performance deviation > threshold
* Build trend report every sprint

----

h3. *8. Process & Governance*

Ascensus currently runs ad-hoc performance testing.
We must implement *full governance*:

* Define “When to request performance testing”
* Define request template
* Create performance testing intake form
* Add performance testing as part of *Definition of Ready* for API & UI changes
* Add performance sign-off to *Definition of Done*
* Create SLA:
** Small change → 2 days
** Medium → 5 days
** Large → 8–10 days
* Create approval workflow
* Train Dev, QA, and PM teams on the new process

----

h3. *9. Documentation*

Priti will maintain a *Performance Testing Knowledge Base* including:

* Setup instructions
* Framework architecture
* Execution steps
* Adding a new load test
* Reading reports
* Troubleshooting common issues
* CI/CD failure analysis guide
* Best practices library

This is added to *Confluence → QA Automation → Performance Testing Hub*.

----

h3. *10. Backlog Growth & Continuous Improvement*

The EPIC remains open for:

* Adding new scripts
* Optimizing performance baselines
* Increasing coverage
* Supporting Partner API load tests
* Supporting new UE features

----

h1. 🎯 *Epic Acceptance Criteria*

* ✔ Priti can independently run, maintain, and debug all performance test scripts
* ✔ IDP deadlock suite fully transitioned & owned
* ✔ Jenkins + GitLab CI/CD integration completed for performance regression
* ✔ New regression performance package created & confirmed stable
* ✔ Performance documentation built and published
* ✔ Governance model implemented and reviewed by QA leadership
* ✔ Teams trained on performance request workflow
* ✔ Performance tests executed as part of defined deployment schedules
* ✔ Reporting dashboards active (Splunk + New Relic + HTML reports)
