column1: API Automation Framework Setup, Design, Maintenance & Governance
column34:
Establish a robust, maintainable, and CI/CD-integrated API automation framework for Ascensus in Stage 1 environment.
This Epic covers end-to-end setup — including environment configuration, regression suite creation, CI pipeline integration, monitoring, documentation, and release governance.

----

The objective of this Epic is to *set up, standardize, and institutionalize API testing within QA Automation* — ensuring that every API module (starting with _Account APIs_) is validated as part of release readiness.

*Scope of this Epic:*

* Local environment setup for all API automation repositories.
* Build lightweight and scalable *API Regression Framework* integrated into GitLab pipelines.
* Create nightly API regression jobs with reporting and monitoring.
* Establish standardized *Confluence documentation*, *API QA guidelines*, and *Definition of Done (DoD)* for all API-related work.
* Integrate API validation into the DevOps CI/CD process, ensuring no release occurs without QA API sign-off.

h2. *High-Level Goals*

||Goal||Description||
|# *Framework Setup*|Establish a stable and reusable API automation framework using Rest Assured + TestNG + Maven, aligned with existing UI automation standards.|
|# *CI/CD Integration*|Create GitLab CI pipelines for API regression with configurable switches for modules.|
|# *Regression & Coverage*|Implement nightly regression suite and module-level execution (starting with Account APIs).|
|# *Metrics & Reporting*|Integrate API results into qTest, TestNG, and Jenkins dashboards.|
|# *Documentation & Governance*|Build Confluence structure covering onboarding, test strategy, DoD, and release readiness process.|

h2. *Key Deliverables*

* API Automation repository setup (local & GitLab)
* Regression XML runners (modular by service)
* CI/CD pipeline for nightly execution
* Confluence pages for process and documentation
* API test reporting integration with qTest
* Regression metrics (pass/fail, coverage %, SLA compliance)
* Defined DoD for API testing within QA sign-off process

h2. 🧱 *Story Breakdown (Create as Child Stories under the Epic)*

h3. *1. Environment Setup & Repository Access*

*Description:*
Grant all required GitLab, Jenkins, and database access for API testing setup. Configure local development environment for API automation execution.

*Acceptance Criteria:*
✅ Sunil has access to GitLab API repos and environment variables.
✅ Postman, Rest Assured, and required dependencies configured locally.
✅ Local API smoke tests run successfully using stage endpoints.

*Tags:* {{@setup}}, {{@access}}, {{@api}}, {{@primev3}}

----

h3. *2. API Framework Baseline Creation*

*Description:*
Build or extend the existing API automation framework using *Rest Assured + TestNG + Maven*.
Define structure for environment configs, test data, and request/response validation.

*Tasks:*

* Create reusable request/response classes.
* Add global config file for Stage1, Stage2, and CAT environments.
* Create base test class with token retrieval and setup/teardown hooks.
* Validate sample smoke API (Account Summary API).

*Acceptance Criteria:*
✅ API framework code structure committed to GitLab.
✅ Base test classes and utility layers implemented.
✅ Able to run Account Summary API smoke test successfully.

*Tags:* {{@framework}}, {{@restassured}}, {{@prime}}, {{@cat}}

----

h3. *3. Account API Automation (Phase 1 – Priority Module)*

*Description:*
Develop and validate test cases for the *Account API* endpoints (Account Summary, Balances, Contributions, Withdrawals).
Cover both positive and negative cases.

*Acceptance Criteria:*
✅ CRUD endpoints automated with Rest Assured validations.
✅ Test data handled via external JSON/YAML files.
✅ Results published to TestNG and qTest.

*Tags:* {{@api}}, {{@account}}, {{@phase1}}, {{@regression}}

----

h3. *4. Pipeline Creation & Integration (DevOps Collaboration)*

*Description:*
Create a dedicated *GitLab CI pipeline* for API regression.
Introduce a new switch (e.g., {{api_regression}}) to control API suite execution.

*Tasks:*

* Create {{api-regression.yml}} pipeline file.
* Integrate with environment variables and secrets.
* Add daily trigger in Jenkins/GitLab scheduler.
* Validate successful execution and report generation.

*Acceptance Criteria:*
✅ Pipeline created and triggered successfully.
✅ Reports accessible via GitLab artifacts and Jenkins.
✅ Logs captured and archived.

*Tags:* {{@pipeline}}, {{@devops}}, {{@integration}}, {{@api}}

----

h3. *5. API Regression Suite (Nightly Execution)*

*Description:*
Develop and maintain nightly regression XML runner for API suites.
Include Account APIs first, followed by other modules (User, Transaction, Beneficiary, etc.).

*Acceptance Criteria:*
✅ Regression XML created for each API module.
✅ TestNG integration complete.
✅ Nightly job scheduled and monitored.
✅ Results shared in daily regression Teams post.

*Tags:* {{@regression}}, {{@nightly}}, {{@api}}

----

h3. *6. Reporting, Metrics & qTest Integration*

*Description:*
Integrate API test execution results with *qTest*, enabling visibility for QA, Dev, and management.
Capture metrics: pass rate, coverage %, API SLA compliance, and defect linkage.

*Acceptance Criteria:*
✅ API suite results published to qTest.
✅ Confluence dashboard updated with weekly metrics.
✅ Regression metrics auto-generated post-run.

*Tags:* {{@metrics}}, {{@reporting}}, {{@qtest}}

----

h3. *7. Confluence Documentation & Best Practices*

*Description:*
Create structured documentation for the entire API QA lifecycle.
Include setup guides, process checklists, and troubleshooting sections.

*Confluence Pages:*

* API Test Setup Guide
* API Regression Execution Guide
* API Test Data Management
* API DoD (Definition of Done)
* API QA Sign-off Checklist

*Acceptance Criteria:*
✅ Confluence documentation completed and published.
✅ DoD and API release checklist approved by QA Lead.
✅ Linked from JIRA Epic for traceability.

*Tags:* {{@documentation}}, {{@dod}}, {{@process}}, {{@governance}}

----

h3. *8. API QA Sign-Off & Governance Process*

*Description:*
Define and enforce a *Definition of Done (DoD)* process for API testing.
Ensure that no API changes move to production without QA sign-off.

*Acceptance Criteria:*
✅ API DoD created and reviewed.
✅ QA sign-off made mandatory in JIRA workflow.
✅ Dev/QA ownership mapped per API service.

*Tags:* {{@governance}}, {{@dod}}, {{@release}}, {{@api}}

----

h3. *9. Maintenance & Continuous Monitoring*

*Description:*
Monitor API regression executions and maintain stability.
Handle flaky tests, update tokens, and manage endpoint changes proactively.

*Acceptance Criteria:*
✅ Nightly job health monitored daily.
✅ Failed runs analyzed within 24 hours.
✅ Summary shared with Swapnil via Teams/email.

*Tags:* {{@maintenance}}, {{@monitoring}}, {{@regression}}

----

h3. *10. Future Scope (Optional / Stretch Goals)*

* Extend to Performance API Testing (Taurus/JMeter integration).
* Add Service Contract Testing (Schema Validation).
* Integrate with Swagger or Postman Collections auto-import.
* Enable parallel execution for API regression.

h2. *Deliverables Summary*

||Deliverable||Target Date||Owner||
|Framework Setup & Smoke Tests|Jan 2026|Sunil|
|Account API Automation|Feb 2026|Sunil|
|CI/CD Pipeline|Mar 2026|DevOps + QA|
|Regression Suite Stabilization|Apr 2026|QA Team|
|Documentation & Governance|May 2026|Swapnil + Sunil|

column1: Environment Pipeline Proj - Metadata API Automation & CI/CD Enablement
column34:
h3. *Epic Summary / Objective*

Establish a *stable, environment-agnostic, CI/CD-integrated Metadata API regression suite* that provides a trusted nightly quality signal for shared upstream Metadata services, with phased expansion based on SME-approved business scenarios.

----

h2. 📌 Problem Statement (Why This Epic Exists)

Metadata APIs are shared upstream services impacting multiple domains (UI, Account, Enrollment).
Current validation is:

* Environment-coupled
* Heavily dependent on static test data
* Fragile after DB refreshes
* Not pipeline-driven

This leads to:

* Late defect discovery
* Inconsistent validation
* High maintenance overhead
* Unclear ownership and confidence gaps

----

h2. 🎯 Epic Goals

* Build *resilient Metadata API automation*
* Remove tight coupling to static environment data
* Enable *parameterized CI/CD execution*
* Establish a *trusted nightly regression baseline*
* Expand coverage in a controlled, SME-approved manner

----

h2. 🧱 Scope of Work

h3. *IN SCOPE*

* Metadata API automation (read-only / GET APIs)
* Dynamic test-data strategy
* Pipeline parameterization (env, branding)
* QC4 + Stage1 execution
* CI/CD scheduled regression
* SME-driven Phase-2 expansion

h3. *OUT OF SCOPE*

* UI testing
* Performance / load testing
* Write APIs (POST/PUT/DELETE)
* Downstream consumer validation
* Manual execution flows

----

h2. 🛠 Phase Breakdown

h3. *Phase-1: Baseline Enablement*

*Target:* End of Year

*Objective:*
Establish a *green, trusted baseline* for Metadata API regression.

*Deliverables*

* Existing Metadata API test suite stabilized
* Dynamic data strategy introduced to reduce static dependencies
* MAP/login-dependent tests isolated or redesigned
* Single scheduled GitLab pipeline enabled
* Same suite runs in QC4 & Stage1
* Failures classified (product / data / external / test)

*Exit Criteria*

* Nightly pipeline consistently green
* Failures are actionable and trusted
* No hidden dependencies on environment-specific data

----

h3. *Phase-2: Regression Expansion*

*Target:* End of January

*Objective:*
Expand regression based on *business-critical, SME-approved scenarios*.

*Deliverables*

* SME + Architect QA working sessions
* Fee, waiver, branding rules finalized
* Disabled tests revisited and enabled where applicable
* Incremental scenario addition (no pipeline changes)
* Coverage measured against approved scope (not test count)

----

h2. 🔄 Key Technical Improvements (Explicitly Tracked in Epic)

h3. *Dynamic Test Data Strategy*

* Reduce reliance on hardcoded accounts
* Prefer derived, query-driven, or rule-based data
* Tests resilient to DB refreshes

h3. *MAP / Login Handling*

* MAP-dependent scenarios currently disabled
* Strategy to re-enable using dynamic or derived credentials
* Avoid fixed user/account dependency

h3. *Pipeline Parameterization*

* Environment passed as pipeline variable
* Branding passed as pipeline variable
* Same pipeline executable across environments without code changes

----

h2. ⚙️ CI/CD Integration Model

* Single scheduled GitLab pipeline
* Non-blocking to UI pipelines
* Nightly execution
* Acts as *early warning signal*, not automatic release gate

----

h2. 👥 Ownership & Accountability

h3. *Epic Owner*

* *Swapnil Patil* – Automation Lead (overall accountability)

h3. *Primary Implementation*

* *Automation Squad Team*

h3. *Day-to-Day Technical Owner*

* *Sunil Godiyal* – Senior Automation Engineer

h3. *Pipeline Enablement*

* DevOps
_(Expected effort: ~1 working day once engaged)_

----

h2. 🔗 Reference Links (MUST be added to Epic)

* *Scrum-of-Scrum Integration Page*
[https://confluence.ascensus.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=357192453|https://confluence.ascensus.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=357192453]
* *Metadata API – Regression Strategy (Phase-1 & Phase-2)*
[https://confluence.ascensus.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=357192413|https://confluence.ascensus.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=357192413]
* *Metadata API Documentation Hub*
[https://confluence.ascensus.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=357192322|https://confluence.ascensus.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=357192322]
* *Epic Link*
[https://ascensuscollegesavings.atlassian.net/browse/QA-330|https://ascensuscollegesavings.atlassian.net/browse/QA-330|smart-link] 

h2. ⚠️ Risks & Mitigations

||Risk||Mitigation||
|DB refresh breaks tests|Dynamic data strategy|
|Static account dependency|Derived / parameterized data|
|MAP/login instability|Isolate & redesign login logic|
|External dependency failures|Explicit classification, not suppression|
|Scope creep|Phase-based expansion with SME sign-off|

h2. ✅ Epic Success Criteria

The Epic is considered *successful* when:

* Metadata API pipeline runs nightly
* Environment switching works via pipeline variables
* DB refresh does not invalidate the suite
* Regression signal is trusted by Scrum-of-Scrum
* Phase-2 expansion begins with SME alignment

column1: [DAILY] Automation Regression Health Check (V2 & V3) – 7:30 AM Triage & Reporting - Nov Week 2 (12/1-12/5)
column34:
Every business day at *7:30 AM* (local to offshore), perform a *daily health check* of the *Prime V2* and *Prime V3* regression jobs that finished overnight.
Tasks include: review *qTest* executions and *TestNG* reports, scan failures *by module*, reproduce locally or run a focused re-execution to confirm legitimacy, and communicate results.

* *If all green:* post a short status update to the team channel.
* *If failures:* email the Project Lead (Swapnil) following the bug-handling guidelines with the required evidence (plan, module, test case(s), exception, logs/screenshots, links). Swapnil will open/route defects.

*Goal:* Provide a reliable, single source of truth each morning on regression health (V2 + V3), with actionable failure details.

*Linked Docs:*

* *Bug Handling Process:* [https://confluence.ascensus.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=326751557|https://confluence.ascensus.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=326751557]
* *TestNG (V2) Report Index:* [http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/?C=M;O=D|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/?C=M;O=D]
* *V3 Pipelines (TestNG inside job artifacts):* [https://gitlab.com/ascensus-gs/products/depot/qa-automation/prime-test-automation/-/pipeline_schedules|https://gitlab.com/ascensus-gs/products/depot/qa-automation/prime-test-automation/-/pipeline_schedules]
* *qTest Portal (Project 90985):* [https://ascensus.qtestnet.com/p/90985/portal/project#tab=testexecution|https://ascensus.qtestnet.com/p/90985/portal/project#tab=testexecution]

column1: Legacy ABL Enrollment – Negative and Validation Scenarios for PAB
column573:
The system must display an error when required fields (e.g., beneficiary name, SSN, DOB) are left blank.
The system must validate SSN format and display an error for invalid or duplicate SSNs.
The system must prevent enrollment if the beneficiary is under the minimum or over the maximum eligible age.
The system must display an error if the initial contribution amount is below the plan minimum or above the maximum.
The system must validate address fields and display errors for incomplete or invalid addresses.
The system must prevent enrollment if the user attempts to enroll a beneficiary who already has an active PAB account.
The system must display an error for invalid email or phone number formats.
The system must require acceptance of all mandatory disclosures and agreements before allowing submission.
The system must display appropriate error messages for any failed backend or service validations.
The system must not allow enrollment to proceed if any validation fails, and must retain entered data for correction.
column792: Legacy Enrollment – Negative and Validation Scenarios

CSR user can enter valid data in all required fields for each section.
System validates and saves the entered data successfully at each step.
CSR user can review all entered information before submission.
CSR user can successfully submit the enrollment.
Confirmation page is displayed with enrollment details.
Enrollment is recorded in the database and is retrievable.
No errors or validation failures occur during the positive flow.

column1: Legacy Enrollment – Direct Plans TND for Single Beneficiary
column573:
The user can successfully start the enrollment process for a TND direct plan.
The system displays all required fields for single beneficiary enrollment (e.g., beneficiary name, DOB, SSN, address).
The user can enter valid information for all required fields.
The system validates all mandatory fields and displays appropriate error messages for missing/invalid data.
The user can select only one beneficiary for the enrollment.
The system allows the user to review all entered information before submission.
Upon submission, the system creates a new TND direct plan account for the single beneficiary.
The user receives a confirmation message and enrollment/account number upon successful enrollment.
All enrollment data is saved correctly in the database and is retrievable for future reference.
The enrollment is reflected in the admin/back-office system for further processing.

column1: Onboarding & Environment Setup for Priti – QA Automation Team
column34:
This story covers the onboarding, learning, and environment setup activities for *Priti*, a new member of the *QA Automation Team*.

The goal is to ensure Priti is fully equipped with access, tools, and understanding of the automation frameworks used across *Stage 1, Universal/IDP and sardine.*
The onboarding process follows the *QA Automation Master Onboarding Guide* (Confluence) and includes setup, access provisioning, KT sessions, and initial hands-on practice.

*Mentor / Buddy:* Dinesh Kumar, Venkatesh Mallela
*Manager:*  Swapnil Patil


*Acceptance Criteria:*

✅ Environment setup complete (JMeter+Tauras+Blazemeter).
✅ Access confirmed to all QA tools (JIRA, GitLab, Jenkins, qTest, Confluence, Splunk).
✅ Successfully ran regression suite locally and validated results.
✅ Participated in KT sessions (Performance testing,GitLab repos, Ci/CD using Jenkins, Monitoring tool).
✅ Added at least one new test case or fixed one existing automation test.
✅ Joined QA team ceremonies (standups, refinement, regression review).
✅ Onboarding checklist signed off by mentor and manager.

h2. 🔹 *Subtasks*

----

h3. *1. Environment Setup (Stage 1/ Local Config)*

*Description:*
Set up the local automation environment to run Stage 1 regression/Perf test. Install all required software and configure stage connections.
*Tasks:*

Primary task: 

* Install JMeter, Taurus, Blaze meter.
* Access provisioning steps for Blazemeter and Jenkins.
* Folder structure guidance for test artifacts.
* Clone performance-test-automation from GtiLab
* Config local env setup (Stage 1 etc)
* Access to Jenkins for Performance batch jobs
* Access to Monitoring tool NewRelic/Splunk
* Verify GitLab credentials and SSH key setup

Secondary task (Optional):

* Install Java 17+, Maven, IntelliJ
* Clone QA-Automation repos from GitLab
* Configure local environment properties ({{stage1}}, {{primev3}}, etc.)

*Acceptance Criteria:*
✅ Able to run performance batch job/ locally and through Jenkins.
✅ Able to access all performance resources and verify result in blaze meter.
✅ Access to Stage1 and local Oracle DB confirmed

----

h3. *2. Tool Access & Permissions*

*Description:*
Obtain access to all required QA and DevOps tools.
*Tools:* JIRA, GitLab, Jenkins, qTest, NewRelic/Splunk, Confluence, Postman, Blazemeter .

*Acceptance Criteria:*
✅ User accounts created and verified for all tools
✅ Able to access and view existing regression results, pipelines, and reports
✅ Added to QA Automation group email and Teams channels

----

h3. *3. Knowledge Transfer (KT) Sessions*

*Description:*
Attend structured KT sessions to understand automation framework design, workflow, and DevOps integration.
*KT Topics:*

* Performance Testing Overview (Nick)
* GitLab Branching and Merge Request Workflow (Nick)
* Regression Flow and qTest Reporting (Priyal)
* Splunk Querying and Debugging Basics
* Prime v3 Framework Overview (Optional)

*Acceptance Criteria:*
✅ Attended all assigned KT sessions
✅ Shared key learning notes in Confluence or Teams
✅ Understood test suite execution flow and tagging strategy

----

h3. *4. Practice Tasks*

*Description:*
Perform hands-on practice on the performance testing framework to gain confidence.
*Tasks:*

* Run Performance test locally
* Raise a Merge Request for code review
* Execute Jenkins job and link report to JIRA
* Environmental differences and configuration tips (QA/UAT/PRO).
* Embedded screenshots, YAML examples, and tool-specific tips.

*Acceptance Criteria:*
✅ Performance test executed successfully locally
✅ Merge Request approved and merged
✅ Jenkins job executed successfully
✅ Test results validated in Blaze meter

----

h3. *5. QA Workflow Familiarization*

*Description:*
Familiarize with the QA team processes, cadence, and collaboration structure.
*Tasks:*

* Join QA daily standups, sprint reviews, and backlog refinements
* Bookmark QA Confluence pages, dashboards, and documentation
* Review existing regression reports and bug tracking process

*Acceptance Criteria:*
✅ Participated in QA ceremonies
✅ Knows where to find team dashboards, Confluence docs, and test results
✅ Added to QA distribution lists and communication channels

----

h3. *6. Onboarding Completion & Sign-off*

*Description:*
Conduct final check-in meeting with mentor and QA manager to review onboarding checklist.
*Acceptance Criteria:*
✅ All setup and KT tasks marked complete
✅ Mentor verifies readiness for independent work
✅ Story closed with sign-off comment in JIRA

----

* Screenshot of Jenkins job execution
* Confluence summary of KT learnings
* GitLab MR link to onboarding test case
* Story comment “Ready for Independent QA Tasks” from mentor

column1: Retrospective Action Add Metrics to Team Dashboard
column34:
h3. *Background*

The current flow metrics dashboard provides visibility into team performance but lacks two key insights:

# *Bug creation volume rate per month* – This metric is not currently tracked, making it hard to analyze trends in bug reporting.
# *Root cause documentation for bugs* – When bugs are closed, their root cause is not consistently captured, limiting the ability to identify systemic issues and improve processes.

h3. *Action Items*

* *Enhance dashboard metrics*:
** Add a new metric to track the *monthly creation volume of bugs*.
* *Improve bug closure process*:
** Update workflow to require *root cause documentation* when closing a bug.
* *Integrate root cause data into dashboard*:
** Summarize and display root cause trends in the dashboard for better visibility.

h3. *Goal*

Provide a more comprehensive view of bug trends and underlying causes to enable data-driven decisions for improving quality and reducing recurring issues.

column1: Retrospective Action Item: Enhancing Domain Knowledge
column34:
*Background*
The team expressed a desire to deepen their domain knowledge to improve backlog understanding and delivery quality. However, this initiative is broad, time-intensive, and requires SME involvement, which could impact ongoing delivery.

*Agreed Action*
To make this initiative manageable and targeted, the following steps were agreed upon:

# *Team Lead Responsibility*
#* Compile a short list of key domain topics or areas that would most benefit the team in tackling their current and upcoming backlog items.
# *Team Polling*
#* Share the list with the team via a poll to gather input on interest and perceived value.
#* Use poll results to prioritize topics based on collective interest.
# *Project Lead Follow-Up*
#* For each prioritized topic, the Project Lead will curate relevant reading materials or locate pre-recorded knowledge transfer (KT) sessions.
#* These resources will serve as prerequisites for live KT sessions.
# *Live KT Sessions with SMEs*
#* Only after the team completes the prerequisite materials will a live session with an SME be scheduled.
#* This ensures efficient use of SME time and maximizes session value.

*Goal*
To build domain knowledge incrementally without disrupting delivery, while respecting SME bandwidth and team capacity.

column1: Specific Legacy Enrollment – Advisor Plans (Single Bene) for COA Plan
column573:
User can start a new Advisor Plan enrollment for a single beneficiary.
All required participant (account owner) fields must be validated for presence and format.
Beneficiary section must require and validate all mandatory fields.
Successor section must be completed or explicitly skipped as per plan rules.
Investment selection must allow choosing individual portfolio or custom allocation, with correct validation.
Funding method selection must support all available options (AIP, EBT, Check, Rollover, Payroll) and validate required fields for the chosen method.
Banking information must be validated for funding methods that require it.
Enrollment cannot proceed unless all required sections are completed and validated.
Upon successful submission, a confirmation screen with account details is displayed.
Enrollment voucher must be reviewed and approved as part of the workflow.
All error messages must match expected text for missing or invalid input.
Enrollment data must be persisted and validated in the database post-submission.

column1: [DAILY] Automation Regression Health Check (V2 & V3) – 7:30 AM Triage & Reporting - Nov Week 2 (11/10-11/14)
column34:
Every business day at *7:30 AM* (local to offshore), perform a *daily health check* of the *Prime V2* and *Prime V3* regression jobs that finished overnight.
Tasks include: review *qTest* executions and *TestNG* reports, scan failures *by module*, reproduce locally or run a focused re-execution to confirm legitimacy, and communicate results.

* *If all green:* post a short status update to the team channel.
* *If failures:* email the Project Lead (Swapnil) following the bug-handling guidelines with the required evidence (plan, module, test case(s), exception, logs/screenshots, links). Swapnil will open/route defects.

*Goal:* Provide a reliable, single source of truth each morning on regression health (V2 + V3), with actionable failure details.

*Linked Docs:*

* *Bug Handling Process:* [https://confluence.ascensus.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=326751557|https://confluence.ascensus.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=326751557]
* *TestNG (V2) Report Index:* [http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/?C=M;O=D|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/?C=M;O=D]
* *V3 Pipelines (TestNG inside job artifacts):* [https://gitlab.com/ascensus-gs/products/depot/qa-automation/prime-test-automation/-/pipeline_schedules|https://gitlab.com/ascensus-gs/products/depot/qa-automation/prime-test-automation/-/pipeline_schedules]
* *qTest Portal (Project 90985):* [https://ascensus.qtestnet.com/p/90985/portal/project#tab=testexecution|https://ascensus.qtestnet.com/p/90985/portal/project#tab=testexecution]

column1: Add Advisor Plan to Regression Suite for End-to-End Coverage
column34:
Enhance regression coverage by adding one *Advisor plan* (e.g., *NYA*, *MIA*, or *WVA*) to ensure advisor-based flows (CSR access, contribution, withdrawal, and enrollment) remain represented after removal of ILD/ILB plans.

*Scope / Tasks:*

* Add a new {{<test>}} block for a stable Advisor plan to {{stage1-acct-overview.xml}}.
* Configure environment variables for CSR and Member roles.
* Validate smoke-level scenarios for account overview and contribution modules.
* Confirm results in nightly regression and align with qTest mapping.

*Acceptance Criteria:*
✅ Advisor plan successfully added and validated in regression.
✅ No errors in TestNG or job logs.
✅ Results reflect successful advisor flow coverage.
✅ Documented in “Regression Coverage – Advisor Plans” section of Confluence.

column1: Add Stable ABLE Plan Coverage to Replace ILB in Regression Suite
column34:
Replace *ILB (Illinois ABLE)* plan, which is currently unstable and causing environment timeouts, with a *stable ABLE plan* (e.g., *NYB* or *PAABLE*) to maintain plan-type diversity in the regression suite.

*Scope / Tasks:*

* Comment out ILB {{<test>}} block in {{stage1-acct-overview.xml}}.
* Add a new {{<test>}} section for a stable ABLE plan (NYB or PAABLE).
* Validate login, dashboard, and withdrawal module compatibility for ABLE plans.
* Confirm regression execution success and absence of authorization errors.
* Update Confluence “Regression Plan Coverage – ABLE” section.

*Acceptance Criteria:*
✅ ILB plan disabled in regression.
✅ Alternate ABLE plan (NYB/PAABLE) added and executes successfully.
✅ TestNG and qTest reports show stable coverage.
✅ Documentation updated for reinstatement and environment tracking.

column1: Add Stable Direct Plan Coverage to Replace ILD in Account Overview Regression
column34:
As part of stabilizing the regression pipeline, remove *ILD (Illinois Direct)* plan from the {{stage1-acct-overview.xml}} runner due to flakiness and backfill coverage using a *stable Direct plan* such as *MID (Michigan Direct)* or *OHD (Ohio Direct)*.

*Scope / Tasks:*

* Disable or comment out ILD test block in {{stage1-acct-overview.xml}}.
* Add a new {{<test>}} section for the replacement *Direct plan* (MID/OHD).
* Validate module-level execution (Balance Page, Dashboard load, Account Details).
* Ensure reports and qTest linkage update correctly.
* Document the change under “Pending Reinstatement – ILD” in Confluence.

*Acceptance Criteria:*
✅ ILD test removed from the regression suite.
✅ New Direct plan successfully added and runs cleanly in nightly regression.
✅ qTest & TestNG reports reflect updated plan.
✅ Documentation updated in regression coverage Confluence page.

column1: Onboarding & Environment Setup for New teammates– QA Automation Team
column34:
This story covers the onboarding, learning, and environment setup activities for *Sunil*, a new member of the *QA Automation Team*.

The goal is to ensure Sunil is fully equipped with access, tools, and understanding of the automation frameworks used across *Prime V2, Prime V3, CAT, and Sardine regression environments*.
The onboarding process follows the *QA Automation Master Onboarding Guide* (Confluence) and includes setup, access provisioning, KT sessions, and initial hands-on practice.

*Mentor / Buddy:* Dinesh Kumar, Venkatesh Mallela
*Manager:*  Swapnil Patil]


*Acceptance Criteria:*

✅ Environment setup complete (Prime v3 local + Stage config).
✅ Access confirmed to all QA tools (JIRA, GitLab, Jenkins, qTest, Confluence, Splunk).
✅ Successfully ran regression suite locally and validated results.
✅ Participated in KT sessions (Prime v3, GitLab, qTest, Regression Flow).
✅ Added at least one new test case or fixed one existing automation test.
✅ Joined QA team ceremonies (standups, refinement, regression review).
✅ Onboarding checklist signed off by mentor and manager.

h2. 🔹 *Subtasks*

----

h3. *1. Environment Setup (Prime V3 / Local Config)*

*Description:*
Set up the local automation environment to run Prime v3 regression. Install all required software and configure stage connections.
*Tasks:*

* Install Java 17+, Maven, IntelliJ
* Clone QA-Automation and Prime-Test-Automation repos from GitLab
* Configure local environment properties ({{stage1}}, {{primev3}}, etc.)
* Verify GitLab credentials and SSH key setup

*Acceptance Criteria:*
✅ Local build successful ({{mvn clean test}})
✅ Able to open regression feature files and execute one scenario
✅ Access to Stage1 and local Oracle DB confirmed

----

h3. *2. Tool Access & Permissions*

*Description:*
Obtain access to all required QA and DevOps tools.
*Tools:* JIRA, GitLab, Jenkins, qTest, Splunk, Confluence, Postman, Blazemeter (optional).

*Acceptance Criteria:*
✅ User accounts created and verified for all tools
✅ Able to access and view existing regression results, pipelines, and reports
✅ Added to QA Automation group email and Teams channels

----

h3. *3. Knowledge Transfer (KT) Sessions*

*Description:*
Attend structured KT sessions to understand automation framework design, workflow, and DevOps integration.
*KT Topics:*

* Prime v3 Framework Overview (Swapnil)
* GitLab Branching and Merge Request Workflow (Nick)
* Regression Flow and qTest Reporting (Priyal)
* Splunk Querying and Debugging Basics
* Performance Testing Overview (Optional)

*Acceptance Criteria:*
✅ Attended all assigned KT sessions
✅ Shared key learning notes in Confluence or Teams
✅ Understood test suite execution flow and tagging strategy

----

h3. *4. Practice Tasks*

*Description:*
Perform hands-on practice on the regression framework to gain confidence.
*Tasks:*

* Run regression suite locally
* Identify a known broken test and fix it
* Add a small new test case (with tags {{@regression}} and {{@dailyrun}})
* Raise a Merge Request for code review
* Execute Jenkins job and link report to JIRA

*Acceptance Criteria:*
✅ Regression executed successfully locally
✅ Merge Request approved and merged
✅ Jenkins job executed successfully
✅ Test results validated in TestNG/qTest

----

h3. *5. QA Workflow Familiarization*

*Description:*
Familiarize with the QA team processes, cadence, and collaboration structure.
*Tasks:*

* Join QA daily standups, sprint reviews, and backlog refinements
* Bookmark QA Confluence pages, dashboards, and documentation
* Review existing regression reports and bug tracking process

*Acceptance Criteria:*
✅ Participated in QA ceremonies
✅ Knows where to find team dashboards, Confluence docs, and test results
✅ Added to QA distribution lists and communication channels

----

h3. *6. Onboarding Completion & Sign-off*

*Description:*
Conduct final check-in meeting with mentor and QA manager to review onboarding checklist.
*Acceptance Criteria:*
✅ All setup and KT tasks marked complete
✅ Mentor verifies readiness for independent work
✅ Story closed with sign-off comment in JIRA

----

column1: Legacy Enrollment – Save as Draft for Direct Plan TND
column573:
Acceptance Criteria: Direct Enrollment Save As Draft
1.Save As Draft Button Visibility
The "Save As Draft" button is visible and enabled at every step of the Direct Enrollment process.
2.Data Preservation
When the user clicks "Save As Draft," all data entered up to that point is securely saved.
3.Draft Retrieval
When the user returns to the Direct Enrollment process, they are prompted to resume from their saved draft if one exists.
4.Data Restoration
All previously saved data is accurately restored when the user resumes the draft.
5.User Notification
After saving as draft, the user receives a clear confirmation message indicating that their progress has been saved.
6.Draft Expiry (if applicable)
If drafts have an expiration period, the user is informed of how long their draft will be retained.
7.Multiple Drafts (if supported)
If the system allows, users can view, select, and manage (delete) multiple drafts.

The system must track consecutive failed login attempts for each user.

For each of the first 6 failed login attempts with an invalid password, the user must see an error message:
"The username and/or password you entered is not recognized. Please try again."
3.Account Lockout on 7th Failed Attempt
On the 7th consecutive failed login attempt with an invalid password, the user’s account must be locked.

column573:
Acceptance Criteria
1.Save Progress Option
The enrollment form provides a visible and accessible "Save As Draft" button at all stages of the enrollment process.
2.Partial Data Preservation
When the user clicks "Save As Draft," all entered data up to that point is securely saved.
3.Draft Retrieval
Upon returning to the enrollment process, the user is presented with an option to resume from their saved draft.
4.Data Integrity
All previously entered and saved data is accurately restored when the draft is resumed.
5.User Notification
After saving as draft, the user receives a clear confirmation message indicating that their progress has been saved.
6.Draft Expiry (if applicable)
If drafts have an expiration period, the user is informed of how long their draft will be retained.
7.Multiple Drafts (if supported)
If the system allows multiple drafts, users can view, select, and manage (delete) their drafts.

column1: ❌ [Stage 1] CSR – Update Personal Information Fails at Submit Action (NJD Plan)
column34:
During today’s Stage 1 automation regression run for the *CSR Update Personal Information* module under *Plan NJD*, the test case failed at the *submit action step* for phone/address update.

The following error was observed:

{quote}{{Error! please try again later.}}
{{Your request cannot be completed at this time. Please contact Plan administrator.}}
{{ErrorCode: [This functionality is not available at this time. Please try again later.] [ts2], [10/30/2025 6:49:18]}}{quote}

This issue is reproducible for Plan NJD during CSR workflow. The submit action for updating contact information triggers this failure, even after test setup and database login steps are successful.

----

*Environment:*

* Stage 1
* Plans: NJD (Regression includes NYB, AKB, ILB)
* Test Case File:
{{/qa-automation/unite/testsuites/frontoffice/csr/member/profileanddocuments/feature/PersonalInformation.feature:32}}

----

*Test Evidence & Logs:*

* 🔍 *Execution Log:*
[View Test Case Execution|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20251030/stage1-csr-acct-maintenance/njd.20251030055018954.PersonalInformation.feature.32_testcase_information.txt]
* 📸 *HTML Snapshot:*
[View Screenshot (Submit Failure)|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20251030/stage1-csr-acct-maintenance/njd.20251030055018955.PersonalInformation.feature.32_html_chrome_failedresult.html]
* 📊 *Full Report:*
[View Daily Report – Stage1 CSR|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20251030/stage1-csr-acct-maintenance/]

----

*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Login as default CSR to NJD environment.
# Navigate to member account – Update Phone/Address Info.
# Submit the changes.
# Observe failure message: _"Functionality not available at this time."_

----

*Impact:*

* This issue blocks CSR users from successfully updating profile details via automation.
* Other plans (NYB, AKB, ILB) are not impacted.
* Manual run confirmed the same failure point.
* Root cause is likely *Stage1 environment-related*, pending deeper analysis.
column724:
During the IDP profile update call, the credential for the Unite client ID in the Auth Server database had expired.
Because of this, the IDP endpoint returned an invalid_client error, which caused an exception during the profile update flow.
The issue was configuration-related and limited to the Stage1 environment only.

The expired credential was renewed and redeployed successfully. All subsequent validations across impacted plans (NYD, NYB, ILB, AKB) passed without issues.

* Validated manually across multiple plans (NYD, NYB, ILB, AKB) and automation runs locally — all passed successfully.
* Main Monolith branch is unlocked and stable.

*Marking this bug as Closed.*

column1: IDP - Enhance Prime v3 Framework for Deep Shadow DOM Element Handling in IDP Angular UI
column34:
The *IDP login and account recovery* flows in the Angular-based UI use *Shadow DOM encapsulation*, sometimes with multiple nested layers.
The current *Prime v3 Selenium 4 framework* supports the first-level Shadow DOM, but fails to detect elements nested deeper (e.g., *child-of-child* Shadow roots).

This spike aims to *research and evaluate solutions* for improving deep Shadow DOM element accessibility and identification — enabling automation to handle multi-level Shadow roots without manual workarounds or disabled MFA.

----

h3. *Objectives:*

# Review *current framework implementation* for Shadow DOM support.
# Identify *limitations* in Selenium 4’s Shadow root handling for nested elements.
# Explore third-party libraries or native WebDriver solutions to support:
#* *Recursive Shadow DOM traversal*
#* *Dynamic child/successor element discovery*
# Collaborate with *Sree Pallavi*, & *Nick (Framework Architect)* to assess adding this to the Prime core library.
# Create a *sample POC* demonstrating multi-layer Shadow DOM element detection.

----

h3. *Scope:*

* Applicable to *Prime v3 (Selenium 4)* and *IDP feature set*.
* Includes *login, password recovery, MFA, and error message* verification screens.
* Will evaluate:
** Selenium 4+ shadowRoot
** JavaScript executor approaches
** Custom utility wrappers
column573:
h3. 

✅ Root-cause of deep Shadow DOM locator limitation documented.
✅ POC created showing automation can locate elements inside *multiple nested Shadow DOMs*.
✅ Recommendations presented to Dev/Automation Leads.
✅ Confluence documentation updated with:

* Framework enhancement proposal
* Sample code snippets
* Known limitations / alternative solutions.
✅ Approved approach to be included in a future framework sprint.

column1: IDP - MFA Strategy & IDP Authorization Integration (Prime v3)
column34:
Currently, *IDP login automation* for *Prime v3* relies on *SQL-based authorization* to retrieve and verify user PINs (for MFA).

This spike aims to *research, design, and propose a secure strategy* that allows end-to-end IDP login automation — including MFA verification — *without bypassing authorization using 2FA*.

The outcome should define a *new MFA-enabled automation framework path* that:

# Can find and use *valid test accounts* dynamically.
# Can handle *PIN retrieval* through a controlled and secure method (API, DB proxy, or service).
# Supports *execution from the automation network* under the new MF3 setup.

This is a *research and design effort*, not immediate implementation.

----

h3. *Objectives:*

# Understand the current *IDP MFA flow* (user authentication, PIN generation, validation in SQL).
# Identify potential solutions:
#* Secure *API endpoint* or *service bridge* for PIN retrieval.
#* Controlled *SQL proxy connection* or *replica DB* for test accounts.
#* Use of *mock authentication tokens* for automation-only login.
# Document risks and feasibility of each approach.
# Design an *end-to-end flow* for IDP login + MFA handling (diagram + pseudocode).
# Provide a *POC or test script* validating at least one approach.

----

h3. *Scope:*

* Includes *MFA (PIN generation, validation)* during automation login.
* Excludes UI automation changes or production MFA logic — this is test-environment-only.
* Collaboration required with *Pramod, Nick, Sree Pallavi* and *Dev/Architects*.

----

h3. *Deliverables:*

* *Confluence Document:* “MFA Strategy for IDP Authorization – Prime v3”
** Flow diagram
** Comparison of options (pros/cons)
** Risk and mitigation plan
** POC details (command/tag level)
* *Code POC (optional):* stored under {{/unite/testsuite/login/idp/mfa_poc/}}
* *Next Step Recommendation:* create implementation story once approved, this will correct current development we have in sprint with MFA disable strategy.

----

h3. *References / Links:*

* *TestNG Reports (Prime v3):* [TestNG pipeline schedule|https://gitlab.com/ascensus-gs/products/depot/qa-automation/prime-test-automation/-/pipeline_schedules]
* *SQL Server DB details:* _(to be added)_
* *Stakeholders:* Nick, Pramod, Dharmendra, Abhitosh
column573:
h3. 

✅ Documented analysis of the *current IDP + MFA flow* and infrastructure gaps.
✅ Identified *3 feasible integration options* (API, proxy DB, token-based).
✅ *Architecture proposal* published in Confluence showing data and security flow.
✅ POC demonstrating a *successful login + MFA verification* in automation (test user).
✅ Recommendations reviewed and approved by QA Lead + DevOps for implementation planning.

h3. *Definition of Ready (DoR):*

* Access to existing SQL scripts and sample PIN flow.
* QA automation environment available for connectivity test.

h3. *Definition of Done (DoD):*

* Feasibility analysis completed and reviewed.
* POC executed demonstrating working end-to-end login with MFA.
* Documentation shared and signed off.

column1: [DAILY] Automation Regression Health Check (V2 & V3) – 7:30 AM Triage & Reporting - Nov Week 1
column34:
Every business day at *7:30 AM* (local to offshore), perform a *daily health check* of the *Prime V2* and *Prime V3* regression jobs that finished overnight.
Tasks include: review *qTest* executions and *TestNG* reports, scan failures *by module*, reproduce locally or run a focused re-execution to confirm legitimacy, and communicate results.

* *If all green:* post a short status update to the team channel.
* *If failures:* email the Project Lead (Swapnil) following the bug-handling guidelines with the required evidence (plan, module, test case(s), exception, logs/screenshots, links). Swapnil will open/route defects.

*Goal:* Provide a reliable, single source of truth each morning on regression health (V2 + V3), with actionable failure details.

*Linked Docs:*

* *Bug Handling Process:* [https://confluence.ascensus.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=326751557|https://confluence.ascensus.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=326751557]
* *TestNG (V2) Report Index:* [http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/?C=M;O=D|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/?C=M;O=D]
* *V3 Pipelines (TestNG inside job artifacts):* [https://gitlab.com/ascensus-gs/products/depot/qa-automation/prime-test-automation/-/pipeline_schedules|https://gitlab.com/ascensus-gs/products/depot/qa-automation/prime-test-automation/-/pipeline_schedules]
* *qTest Portal (Project 90985):* [https://ascensus.qtestnet.com/p/90985/portal/project#tab=testexecution|https://ascensus.qtestnet.com/p/90985/portal/project#tab=testexecution]

column573:
When a user attempts to use the Forgot Password feature, KIDS accounts must not be returned or processed.
During Web Registration, KIDS accounts must not be available for selection or update.
All database queries for these features must exclude accounts where the account type is KIDS.
Regression tests must verify that KIDS accounts are not included in any user-facing lists or backend processing for these flows.
If a KIDS account is entered manually, the system should display an appropriate error message and prevent further action.
Audit logs must not record any activity for KIDS accounts in these fl

column1: [Low Priority] CAT Environment Regression Setup – Smoke Suite Creation & Deployment Integration
column34:
As part of the *CAT Build Out project* (targeting repurposing Stage5 as the new CAT environment), QA Automation is tasked with creating and configuring a *minimal smoke-level regression suite* that will execute automatically *only during deployments* to CAT.

The goal is to:

* Establish a *CAT-specific regression suite* across all major modules (Enrollment, Withdrawal, Contributions, Account Maintenance, Balance Page, Login, etc.).
* Ensure *bare minimum test coverage* to validate system stability post-deploy.
* Integrate the smoke suite with *Jenkins* to trigger on deploy completion.
* Route *alert notifications* to *Swapnil Patil* and the *Dev Team* for rapid triage.

This suite will serve as the baseline automation foundation for CAT environment validation.

----

column1: IDP Login -add Login to Lock the user with 7th attempt
column34: Implement an automated test to verify the lockout and redirection behavior of the Identity Provider (IDP) login after multiple failed attempts. The test should ensure that after 6 consecutive failed login attempts with an invalid password, the system displays an error message. On the 7th attempt, even if the credentials are still invalid, the user should not see the error message and should be redirected to the next page (e.g., account lockout or further verification page). This scenario helps validate the security and user experience of the login lockout mechanism.
column573:
1.The test loads the required test cases and navigates to the "login" URL for a "member" user in a browser.
2.The test attempts to log in 7 times using an invalid password for the same user.
3.For each of the first 6 attempts, the system displays the error message:
"Invalid username or password"
4.On the 7th attempt, the error message is not displayed.
5.On the 7th attempt, the user is redirected to the next page (additional verification page).

column1: IDP Login - Add MFA Enabled Login
column34: Implement an automated test scenario to verify that a user with Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) enabled can successfully log in via the Identity Provider (IDP) screen. The test should ensure that after entering valid credentials for an MFA-enabled user, the user is redirected to the PIN request page, indicating that the MFA flow is triggered.
column573:
1. The test loads the required test cases and navigates to the "login" URL for a "member" user.
2. The test uses a browser session to perform the login.
3. When valid credentials for an MFA-enabled user are entered on the IDP login screen, the system should redirect the user to the PIN request page.
4. The test should assert that the PIN request page is displayed (e.g., by checking for the presence of the text "PIN REQUEST" or a unique element on the page).
5. The scenario should be data-driven, allowing for different MFA-enabled users to be tested via the Examples table.

column1: [PRIMEv3] Fix & Validate UV Enrollment Suite – GitLab Pipeline Integration & Code Cleanup
column34:
h3. *Description*:

This story is part of the *Prime v3 pipeline migration* effort, being developed in *GitLab* in collaboration with *Nick* and *Adam*. The objective is to fix and validate the *UV Enrollment Suite*, which is currently being integrated into the new GitLab pipeline. The goal is to ensure that the test suite executes reliably, generates reports, and uploads results to qTest as expected.

Currently, the suite has a few failing scenarios. This story captures the process of reviewing those failures, fixing the code (feature files, step definitions, runner files), ensuring tagging, and validating reporting output.

h3. *Scope of Work*:

* Review existing UV Enrollment feature suite in Prime v3.
* Fix broken step definitions and feature files.
* Ensure all test cases are properly tagged for filtering.
* Generate reliable Surefire reports after execution.
* Ensure qTest integration works for result publishing.
* Coordinate with DevOps/Ops team to:
** Add artifacts to Nexus (JARs/dependencies).
** Update {{.gitlab-ci.yml}} config as needed.
** Fix any environment-specific issues for Prime v3.
* Run manual execution of the suite until green.
* Perform final code check-in to GitLab after successful validation.
column573:
* UV Enrollment suite passes in manual and GitLab-triggered execution.
* All test cases have proper tags ({{@regression}}, {{@uv}}, etc.).
* Surefire HTML report is generated post-execution.
* Test execution results are uploaded to *qTest*.
* Feature files contain a proper description and relevant metadata.
* All artifacts (dependencies/JARs) are available in Nexus for the GitLab pipeline to use.
* GitLab {{.yml}} file is updated if required.
* Final code is reviewed, tested, and merged.

column1: NY ABLE: 'Next' button unresponsive on Contribution page
column34:
During contribution flow for the *NY ABLE plan*, when attempting to submit a contribution using the “Next” button after entering a valid amount and selecting an existing bank account, the screen freezes and does *not proceed* to the next step (Review).

The button remains clickable, but the page does not respond, and there is no error message or transition.

----

h3. 📌 *Steps to Reproduce*

# Log in as a NY ABLE member
# Navigate to *Make a Contribution*
# Enter a valid contribution amount (e.g., {{$455.00}})
# Select *Recurring (Automatic Investment Plan)*
# Choose existing bank account ({{Chase - Jo Mowery - ****3923}})
# Click *Next*

----

h3. ❌ *Expected Result*

User is taken to the *Review* step of the contribution process.

----

h3. 🚫 *Actual Result*

The screen *freezes* on the current step, *"Details"*, and clicking *Next* has *no effect*. The button remains visible but unresponsive. No visible error is shown to the user.

----

h3. 🧪 *Test Data*

{noformat}Account ID: 92625508001
CONTRIB_TYPE: EBT
AIP: Y
INCOMING_FUND_AMT_1-01-1: 455.00
INCOMING_TXN_TYPE: DOLLAR
INCOMING_MULTIPLE: TRUE{noformat}

----

h3. 🔗 *Logs & Evidence*

* ✅ [Execution Report|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250930/stage1-contributions/#]
* 🧊 [Snapshot|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250930/stage1-contributions/nyb.20250930003511552.AbleMemberSingleContribution.feature.31_html_chrome_failedresult.html]
* ❗ [Exception Logs|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250930/stage1-contributions/nyb.20250930003511568.AbleMemberSingleContribution.feature.31_exception_failedresult.txt]
* 📄 [Test Case Information|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250930/stage1-contributions/nyb.20250930003511573.AbleMemberSingleContribution.feature.31_testcase_information.txt]

----

h3. 💻 *Environment*

* *Environment:* Stage1
* *Feature File:* {{AbleMemberSingleContribution.feature:31}}
* *Browser:* Chrome (Headless)
* *Automation Test:* Failed at investment amount submission step
* *Test Case:* {{NYB - Single Contribution – ABLE – Recurring – AIP}}

column1: Sardine Regression – Identify & Automate Missing Scripts for Regression Flows
column34:
Create and configure a *dedicated regression suite* covering *Enrollment* and *Withdrawal* modules across *Legacy*, *NextGen*, *Specific Legacy*, *Universal Enrollment (UE)*, and *Partner API* systems.

As per requirements shared by *Priyal Patel*, the suite must include all missing plan-level enrollments (Legacy, NextGen, Specific Legacy, UE, GSP) and withdrawals, ensuring end-to-end validation for CSR and Member flows.

This task involves:

# Reviewing existing automation ({{stage1-enrollments.xml}} and {{stage1-withdrawals.xml}}) to identify gaps.
# Creating a *new regression XML suite* (module-separated) for missing flows.
# Validating through *GXML execution*.
# Checking in new suite and scripts into *GitLab repo*.
# Creating *Jenkins jobs* to schedule automated runs.

This serves as a *baseline* for Sardine’s QA automation team to take ownership, expand coverage, and manage ongoing regression execution.

----

h3. *Scope / Modules:*

✅ *Enrollment Systems to Include:*

* NextGen Enrollment System ({{enrollment/createEnrollProspect/viewCollect.cs}})
* Legacy Enrollment System ({{uii529enroll/gettingStarted.do}})
* Specific Legacy System ({{dstcolongenroll/gettingStarted.do}})
* Universal Enrollment (UE)
* GSP Enrollment
* Unite Partner API Enrollment

✅ *Withdrawal Coverage:*

* Qualified / Non-Qualified
* Advisor / ABLE / Flywire / Beneficiary
* Negative validation scenarios

column1: QA Automation - Onboarding, Learning & KT for New Team Members
column34:
This Epic captures all tasks related to the *onboarding, Learning, knowledge transfer (KT), environment setup, and skill enablement* of newly onboarded team members in the QA Automation team.

All learning will align with the [QA Automation - Master Onboarding Guide|https://confluence.ascensus.com/display/GSSD/00.+QA+Automation+-+Master+Onboarding+Guide], and will cover both tools and processes used in the Ascensus QA environment.

h3. Goals:

* Accelerate onboarding of new QA team members.
* Ensure smooth handover of domain knowledge, tools, frameworks, and workflows.
* Prepare new hires for regression support, test automation, and daily responsibilities.
* Align team members with DevOps, qTest, GitLab, Performance Testing, and Prime v3 setup.

h3. 👥 Participants:

* *New Team Member(s):* Add name(s) here
* *Mentor(s)/Point of Contact:* Add senior team member or onboarding buddy name
* *Manager:* Add QA Manager name
* *Tech Leads (if any):* Add name

h3. 📚 Reference Link:

🔗 [Master Onboarding Guide (Confluence)|https://confluence.ascensus.com/display/GSSD/00.+QA+Automation+-+Master+Onboarding+Guide]

h3. ✅ Story List / Suggested Subtasks Under Epic:

Create these as individual stories/tasks under this Epic in JIRA:

----

h4. 🔧 Environment Setup

# *Setup GitLab Access and QA Repositories*
# *Install Java, Maven, IntelliJ, and configure Automation repo*
# *Configure Local Oracle & SQL Developer Access*
# *Set Environment Variables (Prime v3, LocalOracle, Stage Config)*
# *Set up TestNG and Regression Suite Debugging*

----

h4. 📂 Tool Access & Logins

# *Access to Jenkins, JIRA, qTest, Splunk, and Confluence*
# *Setup Postman & API Credentials (Unite and Universal)*
# *Configure Blazemeter / Taurus / JMeter (for Performance Team)*

----

h4. 📘 Knowledge Transfer Sessions (KT)

# *KT: Prime v3 Framework Overview*
# *KT: GitLab branching & Merge Request process*
# *KT: Automation Regression Workflow*
# *KT: qTest Folder Structure & Reporting*
# *KT: Splunk Querying Basics*
# *KT: Performance Testing Overview (if applicable)*

----

h4. 🔄 Practice Tasks

# *Run Regression Suite Locally*
# *Fix 1–2 Known Broken Tests (Real-time Practice)*
# *Add a New Test Case to Prime v3 Regression Suite*
# *Practice: Raise MR, Run Jenkins Job, Attach Reports to JIRA*

----

h4. 🗂 Miscellaneous Tasks

# *Bookmark QA Resources, Docs, and Communication Channels*
# *Join QA Standups and Team Meetings*
# *Attend One Cross-Team Demo Session (Optional)*
# *Complete Internal Compliance / Security / Mandatory Training*

----

h3. 📆 Suggested Timeline:

* *Week 1:* Environment Setup + KT Sessions
* *Week 2:* Tool Practice + Regression Execution
* *Week 3:* Independent Tasks + Real-Time Debugging
* *Week 4:* Active Involvement in Team QA Processes

column1: Design and Add CSR Systematic Withdrawal Plan (SWP) Test Cases to Regression Suite
column34:
This story aims to design, implement, and integrate test cases for the *CSR Systematic Withdrawal Plan (SWP)* feature into the daily *Regression Suite*. The test cases will ensure that core SWP flows such as *create, update, delete, and validate auto-notation* are working as expected across multiple payees and withdrawal types.

The feature is already automated and resides in the test suite as:
📁 {{/qa-automation/unite/testsuite/frontoffice/mid/csr/member/swp/feature/CSR_SWP_Auto_Notation.feature}}

----

*✅ Acceptance Criteria:*

# 📘 *Test Case Design:*
#* Review the existing feature file: {{CSR_SWP_Auto_Notation.feature}}
#* Design at least *5 distinct test cases* (test data driven via {{Scenario Outline}}) including:
#** Multiple withdrawal types
#** Payees: Bank, ACH, Check, Distribution AO, etc.
#** Validations post-creation from database and UI
# 📌 *Regression Tagging:*
#* Ensure all applicable scenarios are tagged with {{@regression}}, {{@dailyrun}}, {{@functional}}.
# 🧪 *Test Data Mapping:*
#* Confirm test data is mapped in the test case loader and DB query files.
#* Accounts should support new SWP creation, update, and deletion.
# 🔁 *Database Validation:*
#* Validate SWP entries directly in the DB after performing create/update/delete actions.
#* Match expected values from the test case JSON/yaml.
# 🧵 *Integration:*
#* Add this feature to the appropriate *mid regression run group* in TestNG runner XML.
#* Ensure it’s part of the *daily regression pipeline*.
# 📊 *qTest Integration (if applicable):*
#* Link automated scripts to corresponding test cases in qTest.
#* Update traceability matrix.
# 📝 *Documentation:*
#* Add test case IDs, input values, and expected outputs to Confluence or Test Data Tracker.
#* Document any dependencies or preconditions for the test case to execute correctly.

----

*🧩 Notes from Feature File:*

* The test case uses *Scenario Outline*, dynamically loading data via {{<test case name>}}.
* Feature includes:
** Login
** Account jump
** Deletion of existing SWP
** Creation of new SWP
** Validation from database (auto-notation, SWP record)

----

*📎 Sample Test Case Names:*

* {{SWP_DistributionAO_ByCheck_Monthly}}
* {{SWP_DistributionAO_ByBank_Biweekly}}
* {{SWP_AchTransfer_Quarterly}}
* {{SWP_DistributionAO_ByACH_Prorated}}
* {{SWP_MultipleDistributions_PayeesCheckAndACH}}

----

*🛠️ Technical Implementation:*

* Framework: Java + Selenium + Cucumber + TestNG
* Location: {{.../csr/member/swp/feature/CSR_SWP_Auto_Notation.feature}}
* DB Validation: Using {{get SWP information from database}} + {{validate database to test case}} steps.

column1: 521 Application Down – CSR & Member Portals Not Accessible in Stage1 & QC4
column34:
The *521 application is currently down*. Both *CSR* and *Member portals* are not accessible in *Stage1* (and likely QC4 as well).
This issue blocks all regression and manual test execution. Appears to be caused by a recent change/check-in.

*Error Message on Screen:*

{noformat}Error! Please try again later.
Your request cannot be completed at this time. Please contact Plan administrator.
ErrorCode[This functionality is not available at this time. Please try again later.], [ts2], [09/11/2025 1:46:28]
{noformat}

*Environment(s):*

* Stage1
* QC4

*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Navigate to CSR or Member portal in Stage1.
# Attempt login or access any module.
# Observe error on screen.

*Expected Result:*
CSR and Member portals should load successfully and allow navigation across modules.

*Actual Result:*
Application fails with “Error! Please try again later.” message.

*Impact:*

* Entire regression suite is failing.
* CSR and Member portals fully blocked.
* No functionality can be tested in Stage1/QC4.

*References:*

* [Regression Report – Stage1 09/11/2025|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250911/]
* [Exception Log|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250911/stage1-acct-overview/nyd.20250911004659318.BalancePageValidation.feature.31_exception_failedresult.txt]
* [Specific Module Report|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250911/stage1-acct-overview/#]
* [Test Case Info|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250911/stage1-acct-overview/nyd.20250911004659322.BalancePageValidation.feature.31_testcase_information.txt]
* [HTML Screenshot|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250911/stage1-acct-overview/nyd.20250911004659322.BalancePageValidation.feature.31_html_chrome_failedresult.html]

*Suggested Next Steps:*

* Investigate recent code/config changes that may have impacted Stage1/QC4.
* Validate DB or infra-level changes.
* Provide ETA on resolution.
column724:
The application threw a java.sql.SQLException: Invalid column name during login for accounts with multiple beneficiaries.
This occurred due to an incorrect alias reference in the SQL query fetching beneficiary data.
As a result, the application failed to retrieve the required data and showed a generic error:

“This functionality is not available at this time. Please try again later.”

🔧 Correction:
* The development team fixed the alias in the backend SQL query.
* Change Sets:
* MR #2741
* MR #2743
* Deployment to Stage1 completed.
* Verified via:
*     Manual retesting of login with affected account.
*     Rerun of relevant regression test cases (Make a Withdrawal flow).

🏁 Resolution Confirmed By: QA Automation Team & Manual Regression Review

column1: [UNITE] Enrollment Failure – "Age-Based" Investment Option Missing in Stage1 for NYD NextGen Multiple Beneficiaries with AIP
column34:
Automated test case failed during the _"Investment selection for first beneficiary"_ step in the *NextGen Enrollment – Multiple Beneficiaries with AIP* flow for NYD plan in Stage 1.

The test failed due to the absence of expected age-based investment fund options (CSS selector: {{div[data-option-content='1003041']}}). As a result, all subsequent enrollment steps were *skipped*.

h3. 🔍 *Failure Snapshot / Logs*

* *Snapshot (HTML)*: [View Snapshot|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250904/stage1-enrollments/nyd.20250904024719194.NextGenEnrollmentMultipleBeneficiary.feature.42_html_chrome_failedresult.html]
* *Exception (Stack Trace)*: [View Exception|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250904/stage1-enrollments/nyd.20250904024719235.NextGenEnrollmentMultipleBeneficiary.feature.42_exception_failedresult.txt]
* *Test Case Info*: [View TestData|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250904/stage1-enrollments/nyd.20250904024719236.NextGenEnrollmentMultipleBeneficiary.feature.42_testcase_information.txt]

h3. 🧠 *Root Cause Analysis (RCA)*

* *Missing UI Element*: The expected {{div[data-option-content='1003041']}} element (used for selecting age-based fund) *was not present* on the investment page.
* *No Age-Based Funds Loaded*: The fund mapping for age-based investment was *not present* or *not loaded correctly* in Stage 1 for NYD plan.
* *Data Issue*: Likely due to incomplete or outdated investment fund configuration for NYD in Stage 1 environment.

h3. ✅ *Resolution / Fix Recommendation*

* Temporarily update test data to *use "Custom Portfolio"* investment option instead of "Age-Based".
* Backend Dev/DB Team to *verify and fix age-based fund availability* (Fund ID: {{1003041}} or related mapping) in *Stage1*.
* Test team to rerun and validate the enrollment once funds are visible.

h3. 🧪 *Next Steps*

* DevOps to confirm data refresh or fund availability for NYD in Stage1.
* QA to rerun the test after confirmation.
* Update test logic to fallback to "custom portfolio" if default option missing (optional).
column724:
*Automation change missed for NYD*

Exception: NoSuchElementException
Failed Selector: div[data-option-content='1003041']
Reason:
The age-based investment fund with ID 1003041 was not present in the DOM at the time of selection. This is likely due to:
* Fund 1003041 not being available in the current fund for NYD.
* Age based funds are missing for NYD


✅ Resolution & Fix Implemented:
* Replaced the age-based investment selection step with a custom portfolio that is reliably available across environments for NYD.
* Ensured available fund options are fetched dynamically from DB before test execution.
* Updated test data setup to reflect only valid fund IDs based on plan configuration.

column1: Stage1 - CSR > Member > Withdrawal Fails with JSPException on Payee Page (Plan: NYD)
column34:
Automation regression is failing on the *Make a Withdrawal* flow via CSR → Member path due to a backend exception rendering the Payee screen.

This appears to be an application-side issue preventing both manual and automation interaction with the withdrawal flow for NYD/ plan accounts.

h3. 📍 *Environment*:

* *Stage 1*
* *Plan*: NY Direct (NYD) Or Michigan Direct Plans
* *Account*: {{388805729-02}}or {{A2572247203}}

h3. ❌ *Error Message Displayed (UI)*:

{noformat}Error: please try again later.
Java.io.IOException: JSPException including path '/cs529/txn/wdPayee.jsp'.
ErrorCode[TThis functionality is not available at this time. Please try again later.], [st1], [08/15/2025 1:54:54]
{noformat}

Screenshot attached below for reference (see image uploaded).

h3. ⚙️ *Test Execution Details*:

*Test Runner*:
{{/qa-automation/unite/testsuite/frontoffice/mid/csr/member/transactions/withdrawal/feature/QandNQDistribution.feature:34}}

*Scenario*: (Most of the CSR WDWL test cases failed) here is one of the scenario
{{Q and NQ Perform Member CSR Distribution Non-Qualified to Distribution AO by Bank with Partial and Prorated}}

h3. 🚦*Step where it fails*:

{noformat}user makes a distribution - Withdrawal Information Non-Qualified to Distribution AO by Bank with Partial and Prorated (FAILED)
{noformat}

Subsequent steps such as selecting payee, amount, and validation are *skipped* due to the failure.

h3. 🔗 *Evidence Links*:

* *Exception Log*: [View|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250815/stage1-withdrawals/mid.20250815005526844.QandNQDistribution.feature.34_exception_failedresult.txt]
* *Screenshot*: [View|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250815/stage1-withdrawals/mid.20250815005526797.QandNQDistribution.feature.34_html_chrome_failedresult.html]
* *Testcase Info*: [View|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250815/stage1-withdrawals/mid.20250815005526847.QandNQDistribution.feature.34_testcase_information.txt]
* *TestNG Summary*: [View Report|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250815/stage1-withdrawals/#]



h3. 📌 *Recommendation*:

Request backend and application support team to review {{/cs529/txn/wdPayee.jsp}} handling and investigate plan-level or account-level configurations that could be triggering the failure.
column724:
*Issue Summary:*
The Make a Withdrawal test case failed in the CSR > Member flow due to a JSP compilation error triggered by incorrect syntax in the wdPayee.jsp file.

*Root Cause:*
A syntax error was introduced in the wdPayee.jsp file during a recent code change. Specifically, the attribute data list="wdTypes" was incorrectly used instead of the correct attribute list="wdTypes" in the <cs:uniteradio> tag.

This caused the JSP to fail during rendering, breaking the withdrawal flow and causing automated regression failures.

*Impact:*
* Automation tests were blocked due to a failure in the main branch.
* CSR and Member withdrawal features could not be verified in Stage 1.
* Delay in regression pipeline for daily verification.

*Resolution:*
* The typo was corrected in the JSP file.
* Code was committed to feature branch and then merged into the main branch.
* Manual validation confirmed successful page rendering and restored flow.

*Change References:*
* MR #2741 – Typo fix in wdPayee.jsp
* MR #2743 – Merge fix into main

column1: Withdrawal Flow: Add Negative Test Scenarios (CSR & Member)
column34:
To strengthen the robustness of the withdrawal functionality, this story focuses on automating *negative test scenarios* across both *CSR* and *Member* channels. These tests ensure that the system gracefully handles edge cases, prevents invalid operations, and shows appropriate error messages or UI behavior.

----

h3. 🔍 *Negative Scenarios to Include*:

* ❌ *Zero balance accounts* – Withdrawal should not proceed.
* ❌ *Accounts with locked or restricted withdrawals* – Check for proper messaging or blocked UI.
* ❌ *Over-withdrawal attempts* – Attempting to withdraw more than available.
* ❌ *Unverified payee details* – EFT or check routing errors.
* ❌ *Closed, inactive, or suspended accounts* – Withdrawal action should be disabled.
* ❌ *Missing account mapping or incomplete registration* – Show validation error.

----

h3. 🎯 *Goal*:

* Ensure that invalid or edge-case scenarios are flagged correctly and do not falsely pass.
* Prevent flakiness by accounting for UI elements that may not appear for invalid accounts.

----

h3. 📂 *Test Areas Affected*:

* CSR Flow – {{stage1-withdrawals.xml}}
* Member Flow – {{stage1-member-withdrawals.xml}}

----

h3. ✅ *Acceptance Criteria*:

* Implement at least 4–5 high-priority negative test cases per flow (CSR and Member).
* Add pre-checks in test setup to ensure accounts are valid for the negative use case.
* Validate appropriate error handling (UI and backend) for each case.
* Test failures must be actionable—not flaky or data-related.

column1: Withdrawal Flow: Add Support for Multiple Plans (CSR & Member)
column34:
The current Make a Withdrawal flow in automation primarily supports a limited set of plan types. To ensure the test suite reflects production-like diversity, this story aims to *extend withdrawal automation coverage* to include multiple plan types across both the *CSR* and *Member* channels.

----

h3. 🔁 *Plan Types to Include*:

* 🟢 *IL Direct Plan*
* 🟢 *IL ABLE Plan*
* 🟢 *Advisor Plans* (varied issuing states)
* 🟢 *Default 529 Plan* used in QA
* 🟢 Any other *unique or complex plans* identified in Stage1/production data

----

h3. 🎯 *Goals*:

* Ensure withdrawal logic (UI flow + backend validations) is consistent across different plan structures.
* Validate plan-specific behaviors such as:
** Available withdrawal types (Qualified/Non-Qualified, etc.)
** Payee options
** Special routing logic or display conditions

----

h3. 📂 *Test Areas Affected*:

* CSR Flow – {{stage1-withdrawals.xml}}
* Member Flow – {{stage1-member-withdrawals.xml}}

----

h3. ✅ *Acceptance Criteria*:

* Add at least 3–5 additional plan types to withdrawal flow (CSR + Member).
* Verify UI behavior, backend updates, and automation consistency.
* Validate successful execution of withdrawal steps for all added plans.
* Update test data configuration and tagging where applicable.

column1: MEM WDRWL: [Research] Investigate Missing 'Send Funds To' Element in CSR Student Loan EBT Prorated Flow
column34:
In the CSR flow for *K12 Apprenticeship and Student Loan Distribution*, the test for *“Pay Student Loan – EBT Prorated”* intermittently fails during the step:
*"Whom should we send the funds?"* due to a missing or non-rendered DOM element.

h4. ❌ *Observed Error*:

{{java.lang.AssertionError: Expected condition failed: waiting for presence of element located by: By.id: form_wdStdloanQualPayeeAO_EFT }}

This indicates that the *dropdown or radio button element* expected to determine the recipient of funds is not available for some accounts or under specific conditions.

----

h3. 🔍 *Test Context*:

* *Scenario*: Perform Member CSR Distribution – Pay Student Loan (EBT Prorated)
* *Test Suite*: _Regression Test Suite – Withdrawals_
* *Runner*: {{stage1-withdrawals.xml}}
* *Feature File*: {{/qa-automation/unite/testsuite/frontoffice/csr/member/transactions/withdrawal/feature/K12Distribution.feature:44}}

----

h3. 📎 *Supporting Evidence*:

* 📸 [Snapshot (UI failure)|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250801/stage1-withdrawals/nyd.20250801010859151.K12Distribution.feature.44_html_chrome_failedresult.html]
* 📄 [Exception Log|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250801/stage1-withdrawals/nyd.20250801010859200.K12Distribution.feature.44_exception_failedresult.txt]
* 🧾 [Test Case Metadata|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250801/stage1-withdrawals/nyd.20250801010859204.K12Distribution.feature.44_testcase_information.txt]

----

h3. 🎯 *Goal of This Research*:

* Determine *why* {{form_wdStdloanQualPayeeAO_EFT}} *is not available* in some runs.
* Validate if certain accounts are *ineligible for EBT Prorated Student Loan withdrawal*, and thus, *UI elements are conditionally rendered*.
* Propose filtering criteria to *avoid selecting ineligible accounts* for this flow.

----

h3. ✅ *Acceptance Criteria*:

column1: MEM WDRWL: [Research] Filter Out Ineligible Accounts Causing Element Not Interactable in Withdrawal Flow
column34:
During execution of the *Qualified Member Withdrawal to BENE (partial balance)* scenario via *Web Registered accounts*, the automation test intermittently fails with the following error:

{{java.lang.AssertionError: element not interactable }}

This issue arises when the locator (e.g., withdraw button, form field, or dropdown) expected by the test *does not exist or is not interactable* for certain accounts. These accounts should not be selected for this flow.

----

h3. 🔍 *Error Context*:

* *Test Name*: {{User Login using Web Registered Accounts for Qualified withdrawal to BENE with partial balance opted}}
* *Test Suite*: _Regression Test Suite – Withdrawals_
* *Runner*: {{stage1-withdrawals.xml}}
* *Feature File*: {{/qa-automation/unite/testsuite/frontoffice/common/member/transactions/withdrawal/feature/MemberWithdrawal.feature:22}}

----

h3. 📎 *Supporting Evidence*:

* 📸 [UI Snapshot|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250801/stage1-withdrawals/nyd.20250801011537822.MemberWithdrawal.feature.22_html_chrome_failedresult.html]
* 📄 [Exception Log|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250801/stage1-withdrawals/nyd.20250801011537863.MemberWithdrawal.feature.22_exception_failedresult.txt]
* 🧾 [Test Case Metadata|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250801/stage1-withdrawals/nyd.20250801011537864.MemberWithdrawal.feature.22_testcase_information.txt]

----

h3. 🎯 *Goal of This Research*:

* Investigate *why certain accounts* do not display the required UI components.
* Identify *business or system rules* that govern whether withdrawal UI elements are present (e.g., account status, plan eligibility, balance constraints).
* Enhance the *account selection query or test data filtering* logic to exclude ineligible accounts from being picked during automation.

----

h3. ✅ *Acceptance Criteria*:

*  Analysis completed on why certain accounts cause non-interactable element errors.
*  Document eligibility rules that affect withdrawal capability.
*  Recommendation proposed to refine SQL or test data logic to filter out these accounts.
*  Optional: Create follow-up story for implementing data filtering or test enhancement.

column1: ACCT Maintenance:  Add more plans & test cases
column34:
The automation scenario for updating delivery preferences via CSR is intermittently failing when the *Edit* button is not present on the UI, indicating the account is *not eligible* to update delivery preferences.

!image-20250801-124110.png|width=813,height=592,alt="image-20250801-124110.png"!



To handle this, a *custom failure message* was introduced:

{{java.lang.RuntimeException: Delivery preference is not editable for this account. }}

However, this does not resolve the root issue and leads to non-actionable test failures in the regression suite.

----

h3. 🔧 *Proposed Solution*:

Enhance the account data retrieval logic to:

* ✅ Add a *pre-check in the SQL or setup step* to ensure only accounts *eligible to edit delivery preferences* are selected for this test.
* Avoid test execution for accounts with locked or read-only delivery preference settings.

----

column1: Balance Page: standardize Decimal Comparison for Account Balance Validation – Avoid String-Based Assertion Failures
column34:
In the *Account Balance Page Validation* test (CSR flow), the regression suite is currently failing due to *string-based assertion* of account balances with differing decimal formats.

h4. 🔴 *Observed Assertion Error*:

{{java.lang.AssertionError: Balance does not match for Positive Balance Account Expected: [1312.7]   Actual: [1312.70] }}

Though both values are numerically equal, the current test performs a *string comparison*, leading to false test failures when decimal formatting differs (e.g., {{.7}} vs {{.70}}).

----

h3. 🔧 *Proposed Resolution*:

Update the assertion logic to:

* *Compare balance values as* {{BigDecimal}} *or formatted* {{Double}}, rather than raw strings.
* Ensure test is resilient to formatting variations that do not affect actual value.

----

h3. 🔍 *Issue Origin*:

* *Feature*: Account Balance Page (CSR)
* *Test Name*: {{Validate member account balance for Positive Balance Account}}
* *Test Runner*: {{stage1-acct-overview.xml}}
* *Framework File*: {{BalancePageStepDefs.java}} (Line 61)

----

h3. 📎 *Supporting Evidence*:

* 📄 *Exception Log*: [View Exception|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250731/stage1-acct-overview/nyd.20250731150139356.BalancePageValidation.feature.31_exception_failedresult.txt]
* 📊 *Test Case Metadata*: [View Test Data|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250731/stage1-acct-overview/nyd.20250731150139357.BalancePageValidation.feature.31_testcase_information.txt]
* 📸 *UI Snapshot*: [View Screenshot|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250731/stage1-acct-overview/nyd.20250731150139358.BalancePageValidation.feature.31_html_chrome_failedresult.html]
* 📈 *Regression Report*: [Full Report|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250731/stage1-acct-overview/#]

----

h3. ✅ *Acceptance Criteria*:

*  Balance comparison updated to numeric comparison (e.g., {{BigDecimal.equals()}} or {{Double.compare()}}).
*  Assertion handles values like {{1312.7}} and {{1312.70}} as equal.
*  Regression suite passes for balance validation test.
*  No impact on other scenarios using numeric validations.
