column1: MEM CONTRIB: Prevent Contribution Attempt When Account Has Reached Annual Limit – Add SQL Eligibility Check
column34:
During regression runs, we observed intermittent test failures when contribution attempts were made against accounts that had already reached the *annual maximum contribution limit (e.g., $19,000.00)*.

This results in UI errors such as:
*"This contribution will cause your account to exceed the annual maximum contribution limit of 19,000.00. Please enter a new amount."*

!image-20250801-122303.png|width=1023,height=353,alt="image-20250801-122303.png"!

----

h3. *Goal*:

Avoid selecting *ineligible accounts* for contribution in automation by filtering them out *at the SQL account selection level.*

----

h3. *Proposed Solution*:

* Add a condition in the *account lookup SQL* logic used for test data generation:
** Only select accounts where the current year’s contribution total is *less than the annual max limit*.
** This prevents contribution steps from failing due to eligibility violations.

----

h3. *Supporting Evidence*:

* *Snapshot* (UI Error Message): [View Screenshot|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250801/stage1-contributions/nyb.20250801003158841.AbleMemberSingleContribution.feature.31_html_chrome_failedresult.html]
* *Exception Log*: [View Exception|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250801/stage1-contributions/nyb.20250801003158870.AbleMemberSingleContribution.feature.31_exception_failedresult.txt]
* *Test Case Info*: [Test Metadata|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250801/stage1-contributions/nyb.20250801003158874.AbleMemberSingleContribution.feature.31_testcase_information.txt]
* *Execution Report*: [Full Report|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250801/stage1-contributions/#]

----

h3. *Test Impact*:

* Contrib automation fails if accounts exceeding the contribution limit are used.
* Skewed false negatives and unnecessary investigation time.
* Test flakiness in both regression and CI pipelines.

----

h3. *Acceptance Criteria*:

* SQL logic updated to exclude over-contributed accounts.
* Manual validation confirms no UI errors on test account selection.
* Tests consistently pass for contribution flows without max-limit violations.

column1: Contribution Flow Failure – JSP Load Error & Missing Element on 'Make a Contribution' Page (EBT - Existing Bank
column34:
h3. 📌 *Description*:

Automation test failed for *“Perform member single contribution for EBT – Existing Bank”* due to a backend JSP error and missing frontend element.

h4. ❗ Observed Behavior:

* JSP page {{/cs529/txn/ebtDetails.jsp}} failed to load during the test.
* UI element {{input[name='amountMap['03']']}} could not be located due to the page not rendering correctly.
* Automation timed out after waiting 30 seconds for the element.
* Page displayed a generic *"Error: please try again later."* message (attached screenshot).

h3. 🔎 *Technical Error Details*:

*Frontend Page Error:*

{{Java.io.IOException: JSPException Including path '/cs529/txn/ebtDetails.jsp' ErrorCode: This functionality is not available at this time. Please try again later. }}

*Selenium Stack Trace (Partial):*

{{org.openqa.selenium.TimeoutException: Expected condition failed: waiting for presence of element... Caused by: org.openqa.selenium.NoSuchElementException: Unable to locate element: input[name='amountM}}

h3. 📁 *Attached Evidence*:

* 🖼️ Screenshot: [View Snapshot|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250731/stage1-contributions/nyd.20250731002925522.MemberSingleContribution.feature.31_html_chrome_failedresult.html]
* 📄 Exception Log: [View Exception|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250731/stage1-contributions/nyd.20250731002925548.MemberSingleContribution.feature.31_exception_failedresult.txt]
* 📊 Test Case Info: [View Details|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250731/stage1-contributions/nyd.20250731002925555.MemberSingleContribution.feature.31_testcase_information.txt]

h3. 🧪 *Steps to Reproduce*:

# Log in to CSR portal.
# Navigate to “Make a Contribution” page for EBT – Existing Bank.
# Attempt to load contribution options.
# Observe backend JSP error and page failure.
# Confirm missing {{amountMap['03']}} input field on the UI.
column724:
*Root Cause:* 
JSP compilation failure due to a missing double quote in a <cs:textfield> tag within the ebtDetails.jsp file.
Offending line:

<cs:textfield ... value="%{#amtMapItem.value}" ← missing " before label=...
Fix Implemented By: Sardine Team

*Verified Via*: Manual validation + successful automation rerun in local

*Branch Status*: ✅ monolith/main branch has been unlocked and automation has resumed

column1: CSR Login Failure – Password Expired Causing Regression Failure in Automation Suite
column34:
Automation regression execution failed across multiple test cases due to *expired CSR login credentials*.

h4. 🔍 Root Cause:

* CSR user login failed due to expired password during automation run.
* Resetting the password via CSR portal resolves the issue (see attached password reset screen).

----

h3. *Exception Logs*:

{noformat}java.lang.Exception: Failed to load/login into csr
	at com.cs529.qa.prime.frontoffice.LoginStepDefs.logInWithUsernamePassword(LoginStepDefs.java:98)
...{noformat}

----

h3. *Impacted Test Cases (Failed)*:

Total Failures: *30*
 Sample Failures:

* TR-8281783: Perform member single contribution for EBT – Existing Bank
* TR-8281784 to TR-8281871 – All CSR-related flows (contribution, withdrawal, enrollment, account info, password update)
* Full list captured in qTest report.

📌 qTest Report: [qTest Link|https://ascensus.qtestnet.com/p/90985/portal/project#id=21717828&object=4&tab=testexecution]
 📷 Screenshot: [Snapshot View|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250722/stage1-contributions/nyd.20250722002608222.MemberSingleContribution.feature.31_html_chrome_failedresult.html]
 📄 Exception Log: [Exception Log|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250722/stage1-contributions/nyd.20250722002608252.MemberSingleContribution.feature.31_exception_failedresult.txt]
 📊 Full Report Dashboard: [Execution Report|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250722/]

----

h3. *Impact*:

* Blocking automation regression suite(54 test cases, 30 failed).
* No functional defects found; failure purely environmental.

----

h3. *Proposed Resolution*:

🔑 Reset CSR user password immediately.
 🔁 Re-run automation after fix to validate all flows.
column724:
*Issue Summary:*
During the scheduled automation regression execution on July 21/22, 2025, 30 out of 54 test cases failed related to CSR module. The failures were not due to application defects, but instead caused by environmental login failure for the CSR test user. CSR User suppose to expire in some days.

*Root Cause:*
* The CSR test account password expired prior to the automation run.
* The login step for all CSR-related flows failed with the exception:

*Detection:*
* Failures were immediately detected via automation test reports and qTest dashboard.
* All failed tests point to login failure with identical stack traces and exception logs.
* CSR portal prompted password update on manual login attempt.

*Corrective Actions:*
* ✅ CSR password was reset manually via CSR portal to match old Password.
* 🔁 Automation regression suite will be re-executed post password reset.
* 📅 Add credential expiration monitoring/reminder to avoid future silent failures.
* 🔒 Implement fallback login check mechanism in the framework to:
*        Flag credential failures upfront.
*        Skip tests gracefully if auth fails at environment level.

*Preventive Measures:*
* Automate CSR credential health check prior to regression execution.
* Configure alert for upcoming password expiry (e.g., 5 days before).
* Maintain environment credential status dashboard.

column781:
25/Jun/25 10:16 AM;6357f972fc0cc7a600ae8914;Hello All,

Following up on the previously reported regression failure related to *NYD Enrollment Submission* in *Stage1* has been resolved and confirmed through a successful regression rerun.

*🐞 Original Bug Summary:*

* *Bug*: [QA-49 – NYD Enrollment Submission Fails – Stage1|https://ascensuscollegesavings.atlassian.net/browse/QA-49]
* *Failure Area*: Enrollment Submit step ([Regression Report – 06/17/2025|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250617/stage1-enrollments/] | [Exception Log|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250617/stage1-enrollments/nyd.20250617014059494.NextGenEnrollmentPositive.feature.143_exception_failedresult.txt])
* *Environment*: Stage1
* *Reported On:* 06/17/2025

*🔧 Resolution Summary:*

* *Root cause*: Code-SQL mismatch — {{uuidPersonId}} field in {{Person.java}} was uncommented without corresponding SQL update, causing JDBC parameter binding issues.
* *Process:* The issue was root-caused and addressed by the respective application team.
* *Fix implemented by*: Sardine team 
* *Verified via*: Daily automation run in Jenkins on 06/25/2025
* *Branch status*: ✅ Main branch unlocked and automation resumed

Thank you all for your support and collaboration in identifying and resolving this issue.

column1: NYD Enrollment failed: due to next business day calculation logic on Prime for long weekend
column34:
NYD Enrollment failed due the wrong next business date calculation during back fund assignment for recurring payment. this issue is in Prime reusable component from framework.

*Test Case*: NextGen Enrollment with Full enrollment options for FUNDID 1003014

*Test NG Report Location*: [http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250522/stage1-enrollments/|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250522/stage1-enrollments/]

*Test Data* : [Here|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250522/stage1-enrollments/nyd.20250522010517762.NextGenEnrollmentPositive.feature.42_testcase_information.txt]
*Exception Details*: [Here|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250522/stage1-enrollments/nyd.20250522010517759.NextGenEnrollmentPositive.feature.42_exception_failedresult.txt]
*Screenshot*: [Here|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250522/stage1-enrollments/nyd.20250522010517728.NextGenEnrollmentPositive.feature.42_html_chrome_failedresult.html]

*Resolution*: 

Fix the date calculation for Next business day

File to fix: \prime\core\ResourceLoader.java @701

column1: Automation Bug: [NYD] Legacy Enrollment – Enrollment flow fails for NextGen flow for Age based fund investments
column34:
During a new scheduled Jenkins pipeline run last night (first nightly run after the setup), we observed a *failure in the NYD Legacy Enrollment regression flow*. Upon investigation, it was found that the failure was *not due to an application defect*, but rather an *automation gap* — the age-based portfolios used in the automation script had been *migrated or closed*, and *NYD should now be using target portfolios*.

h3. ❌ *Expected Result*

Enrollment is submitted successfully, and confirmation page is displayed.

----

h3. ❗ *Actual Result*

Enrollment submission fails with error:
_java.lang.AssertionError: Expected condition failed: waiting for presence of element located by: By.cssSelector: div[data-option-content='1003011']_



*Log* can located: [here|http://seleniumhubnt2:8081/reports/unite/20250515/stage1-enrollments/#]

*Feature file:* {{NextGenEnrollmentPositive.feature}}

*Related Feature File*: {{frontoffice/nyd/member/enrollment/feature/NextGenEnrollmentPositive.feature}}

*Reported By:* Swapnil Patil

*Exception*:

_"java.lang.AssertionError: Expected condition failed: waiting for presence of element located by: By.cssSelector: div[data-option-content='1003011'] (tried for 30 second(s) with 500 milliseconds interval) 	at com.cs529.qa.prime.core.Logger.failTestCase(Logger.java:120) 	at com.cs529.qa.prime.core.Logger.failTestCaseWithScreenShot(Logger.java:45) 	at com.cs529.qa.prime.core.ScenarioManipulator.clickElement(ScenarioManipulator.java:863) 	at com.cs529.qa.unite.frontoffice.member.enrollment.NextGenEnrollmentStepDefs.chooseInvestmentOptionAsAgeBased(NextGenEnrollmentStepDefs.java:1269) 	at com.cs529.qa.unite.frontoffice.member.enrollment.NextGenEnrollmentStepDefs.chooseInvestmentOptionAsAgeBasedFirstBeneficiary(NextGenEnrollmentStepDefs.java:1236) 	at ?.user selects investment as age-based for Full enrollment options with AIP(/qa-automation/unite/testsuite/frontoffice/nyd/member/enrollment/feature/MemberEnrollmentPositive.feature:17)_

column1: Templates: Create application level templates/drafts for ready to use
column34:
Designed reusable templates to *standardize how performance test results* are captured, reported, and communicated across teams for any application in near future requests for the performance test. 

This aligns expectations between QA, Dev, and business stakeholders.



*📄 Template Fields:*

* Feature Under Test
* Test Type
* Environment
* Execution Date/Time
* SLA Thresholds
* DoR & DoD
* Actual Results (response, TPS, error %)
* SLA Met? (Yes/No)
* Attached Logs/Graph

*🧩 Status:* Attached to all recent executions for consistency

*💡 Impact:* Brings transparency, enables side-by-side comparisons, and streamlines communication of performance results.

Documents can be located [*here*|https://confluence.ascensus.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=315555868]
column792: Document and be able to execute current performance testing

column1: Integrate CI/CD Execution for Performance Tests
column34:
Integrate Taurus and JMeter-based performance tests into Jenkins CI pipelines. 

Add scheduled nightly runs and post-deployment triggers to Blazemeter execution reports for critical flows.

*🔧 Configuration Highlights:*

* YAML-based test configs triggered via Jenkins
* Results auto-sent to Blazemeter with test ID mapping
* Email/Teams alerts for failures or SLA breaches
* Post-build actions include log archiving + timestamping



*📅 Owner:* Performance lead + DevOps pairing

*💡 Impact:* Enables continuous validation of performance SLAs during and after releases. Reduces manual test triggering and increases feedback loop speed.



Document can be located [here|https://confluence.ascensus.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=315557408]
column792: Document and be able to execute current performance testing

column1: Document “Definition of Done” for Performance Testing
column34:
Created and published a *Definition of Done (DoD) Template* for any application request for Performance testing, created a checklist specific to performance testing. This ensures every test case or test plan adheres to minimum acceptance criteria before it is considered complete and CI-ready.

*📋 DoD Items Include:*

* SLA targets defined and agreed
* YAML/test plan reviewed and approved
* Results logged with metrics: response time, TPS, error %
* Test execution linked in Confluence or CI pipeline
* Defects (if any) created and traceable in Jira


*💡 Impact:* Introduces process discipline, review consistency, and clear closure criteria for performance engineering tasks.

*📌 Usage:* Attached to every story template going forward. For any application if they request for Perforamance testing to be done then use this page to as template for DoD.



[Confluence Doc: |https://confluence.ascensus.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=315555989]This page defines the *standard operating practices* and *Definition of Done (DoD)* for performance testing efforts.

column1: Migrate Historical Performance Tests to Standard Repo
column34:
Reviewed and consolidated historical performance testing artifacts scattered across PDFs, emails, folders, and legacy JMeter files. 

Team needs to plan to migrated them into a *centralized, organized Git repository*, categorized by feature, test type, and environment.

*🌟 Migration Summary:*

* legacy PDFs/Emails/Docs need to be tagged and archived with execution dates and outcomes.
* Consolidate the Blazemeter & rancher reports to central location for GS, its scattered now
* JMeter {{.jmx}} files converted or documented with Taurus/YAML structure.
* Linked each migrated test to feature + expected SLA (if available).
* Added execution history summaries and test owner details.

*📅 Completion:* End of Week 2, Q2 2025

*💡 Impact:* Enables historical baseline comparisons, supports audits, and prevents loss of tribal knowledge.

column1: Create Feature-Level Risk & Volatility Matrix
column34:
Designed a volatility matrix mapping product modules based on:

* Frequency of change (via Git commits, release notes)
* Defect volume & severity from past 2 quarters
* Business criticality ranking

This matrix now acts as a *risk-based prioritization model* for performance testing not yet designed just drafted a template on how we will be using this henceforth.


*📊 Visuals:* Heatmap and scorecards generated using Confluence tables + charts (TBD)

*💡 Impact:* Informs which areas get full regression, targeted spike/load tests, and alerting focus.

Document can be located [Here|https://confluence.ascensus.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=315557170]

column1: Build Confluence Dashboard for Performance Visibility
column34:
Created a live performance dashboard in Confluence to track real-time and historical test execution results. This enables both technical teams and leadership to monitor SLA health and risk trends.

*🌟 Metrics Included:*

* SLA pass % by environment
* Number of executions per feature
* Failures by module over time
* Environment-wise comparison (QA vs UAT vs PRO)
* Linked defects and flaky test markers


*📌 Schedule:* Updated weekly via Jenkins job linked to Blazemeter data

*💡 Impact:* Brings visibility, accelerates release confidence, and highlights regression-prone areas.

Document can be located [Here|https://confluence.ascensus.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=315557357]
column792: Document and be able to execute current performance testing

column1: Define SLAs and Thresholds for Core Features
column34:
Created a structured SLA definition document mapping each key feature to its expected performance criteria. Defined thresholds will serve as a baseline for test pass/fail criteria in all future test plans.

*🌟 SLA Metrics Include:*

* Avg Response Time
* Peak TPS (Transactions Per Second)
* Error Rate Threshold (%)
* 95th Percentile Response Times

*🌐 Examples:*

* *Login*: ≤ 800ms avg response, TPS ≥ 50
* *Plan Summary*: ≤ 2.5s 95th percentile
* *Dashboard Load*: ≤ 5s with < 1% error rate


*🛡️ Future Use:* Integrated into CI pipeline result analysis & Confluence dashboards.

*💡 Impact:* Enables pass/fail automation, objective measurement, and risk profiling.

Document can be located [Here|https://confluence.ascensus.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=315556628]
column792: Document and be able to execute current performance testing

column1: Standardized YAML Templates for Test Types
column34:
Created a library of standardized YAML templates for different performance test scenarios, designed for quick reuse and compatibility with CI pipelines (Taurus + Jenkins + Blazemeter).

*🌟 Test Types Covered:*

* *Load Test*: Constant load over 30 mins.
* *Stress Test*: Gradual load increase to identify max capacity.
* *Spike Test*: Abrupt traffic surges.
* *Soak Test*: Sustained load for 2+ hours.
* *Baseline Test*: Lightweight, frequent checks for regressions.


*🧩 Reuse:* All future stories can copy/update from these templates directly.

*💡 Impact:* Promotes consistency, reduces creation time, and enforces SLAs per scenario.

Document can be located [Here|https://confluence.ascensus.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=315557160]
column792: Document and be able to execute current performance testing

column1: Create Performance Testing Onboarding & Setup Documentation
column34:
Developed a comprehensive onboarding package to support new and existing performance team members. This document outlines all the necessary steps to get started with performance testing at Ascensus.

*🌟 Key Details:*

* Local setup instructions for Taurus, JMeter, and Blazemeter.
* Access provisioning steps for Blazemeter and Jenkins.
* Folder structure guidance for test artifacts.
* Environmental differences and configuration tips (QA/UAT/PRO).
* Embedded screenshots, YAML examples, and tool-specific tips.



*💡 Impact:* Reduces onboarding time, improves knowledge consistency, and sets foundation for scaling performance testing resources.

Document can be located [Here|https://confluence.ascensus.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=315557345]

column1: Document and be able to execute current performance testing
column34:
*🎯 Objective:* This epic aims to establish a *standardized, transparent, and scalable Performance Testing practice* for the organization. This epic lays the groundwork for future maturity.

*Under this epic, we have:*

* Created best practices and onboarding documentation
* Established a traceable repository of historical and active performance tests
* Designed reporting dashboards and metrics for leadership visibility
* Defined SLAs, test types, execution cadence, and risk-based testing models

The outputs of this initiative now serve as the *foundational framework* for expanding performance engineering capabilities across all product teams. 

----

h3. ✅ What Has Been Done (Q1 – Early Q2 2025):

* *Best Practices & Onboarding Documentation*:
Created end-to-end documentation covering setup, test types, SLAs, toolchain, environments, tagging, and traceability for onboarding current and future team members.
* *Test Repository Structuring*:
Migrated all legacy and current performance test artifacts (PDFs, YAMLs, JMeter plans) into a centralized, versioned repository (Confluence + Git + Blazemeter).
* *Volatility & SLA Mapping*:
Built a volatility matrix for identifying high-change modules and mapped features to test types (e.g., login = baseline + stress, cart = regression + spike).
* *Governance & Reporting Setup*:
Defined a “Definition of Done” for performance testing. Created dashboards tracking SLA pass rates, feature-wise execution, environment-level benchmarks.
* *Tool Integration & Templates*:
Standardized Taurus/JMeter test templates for CI pipelines and integrated Blazemeter dashboards with scheduled Jenkins runs.

----

h3. 🔜 What is Planned (Mid Q2 – Q3 2025):

* *Automation of Monthly Reports*:
Auto-generate performance health reports via JIRA dashboards and Blazemeter API integration.
* *Test Planning Workflow*:
Build JIRA ticket templates that auto-populate fields for performance test setup (SLA, test type, environment, risk score).
* *Expand Traceability Matrix*:
Include direct links from JIRA Epics → Stories → Test Cases → Results → Defects using qTest/JIRA integration.
* *Feature-Level Ownership & Test Suite Versioning*:
Assign performance ownership per feature/module, version test plans with change control via Git or Confluence macros.
* *Baseline Management & Anomaly Alerts*:
Archive performance baseline data and integrate alerts for SLA breaches or anomalous results.

----

h3. 🚀 What Will Be Achieved by End of Q3:

* A fully operational *Performance Testing Center of Excellence (CoE)* framework for QA
* Visibility into test execution, failures, and risk areas
* Traceable, repeatable performance test plans across all high-risk features
* Predictable reporting to support go/no-go decisions
* Compliance-ready historical logs and trend analysis
